   247→
   248→## The Stack
   249→
   250→### Backend — FastAPI
   251→
   252→The backend is a **FastAPI** application using Python 3.11+.
   253→
   254→**Key libraries:**
   255→- `fastapi` — HTTP framework with automatic OpenAPI generation
   256→- `pydantic v2` — Data validation and settings management
   257→- `sqlalchemy 2.0` — ORM for relational data
   258→- `alembic` — Database schema migrations
   259→- `chromadb` — Local vector database for agent memory
   260→- `uvicorn` — ASGI server
   261→
   262→**Why FastAPI over Django/Flask?**
   263→FastAPI automatically generates an OpenAPI spec from your Python type hints. This is the engine behind the entire type synchronization pipeline. Change a Pydantic model → the spec updates → TypeScript types update. Zero manual work.
   264→
   265→### Frontend — Next.js
   266→
   267→The frontend uses **Next.js 16** with the **App Router**.
   268→
   269→**Key decisions:**
   270→- **Server components by default** — only add `"use client"` when interactivity requires it. This keeps the bundle small and SSR fast.
   271→- **`@repo/shared-types`** — Never write API response types manually. Import them from the shared package.
   272→- **`@repo/ui`** — Look here before creating a new component.
   273→
   274→### Shared Types Pipeline
   275→
   276→This is the most important architectural feature. Here is how backend and frontend types stay in sync:
   277→
   278→```
   279→FastAPI Pydantic schemas
   280→         │
   281→         ▼
   282→ openapi.json (generated)
   283→         │
   284→         ▼
   285→ packages/shared-types
   286→         │
   287→         ▼
   288→TypeScript interfaces + Zod schemas
   289→         │
   290→         ▼
   291→ apps/web imports @repo/shared-types
   292→```
   293→
   294→**You never need to write TypeScript interfaces for API responses.** They are generated.
   295→
   296→To trigger the full pipeline after a schema change:
   297→
   298→```bash
   299→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   300→```
   301→
   302→### UI Library
   303→
   304→`packages/ui` is a shared React component library. Add components here when they are used by more than one app. Currently exports `<Button />`.
   305→
   306→---
   307→
   308→## The Skills System
   309→
   310→### What Is a Skill?
   311→
   312→A skill is a bash script that encodes a repeatable, multi-step operation into a single command. It is a shared playbook for both humans and AI agents.
   313→
   314→Every skill lives in `skills/<name>/` and contains three files:
   315→
   316→```
   317→skills/my-skill/
   318→├── manifest.json   # Machine-readable: name, trigger, inputs, risk level
   319→├── README.md       # Human/agent-readable: when and why to use it
   320→└── run.sh          # The actual executable, always run from repo root
   321→```
   322→
   323→Agents discover skills by reading `skills/SKILLS_REGISTRY.md` first. Skills replace the need to remember long command sequences.
   324→
   325→### Available Skills
   326→
   327→| Skill | Run Command | When to Use |
   328→|-------|-------------|-------------|
   329→| `type-sync` | `bash skills/type-sync/run.sh` | After changing any Pydantic schema |
   330→| `db-migrate` | `bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "message"` | After changing a SQLAlchemy model |
   331→| `openapi-gen` | `bash skills/openapi-gen/run.sh` | To regenerate `openapi.json` alone |
   332→| `docker` | `bash skills/docker/run.sh up\|down\|migrate` | Start/stop the full stack (API + Postgres) |
   333→| `memory` | `bash skills/memory/run.sh save\|recall\|list` | Save or recall agent context |
   334→| `checkpoint` | `bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "message"` | At logical milestones (commits) |
   335→| `dependency-add` | `bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh js\|py <pkg>` | Adding any new package |
   336→| `lint-fix` | `bash skills/lint-fix/run.sh` | Before committing |
   337→| `test-run` | `bash skills/test-run/run.sh` | After completing work |
   338→
   339→### When Agents Must Use Skills
   340→
   341→These triggers are **mandatory**, not optional:
   342→
   343→```
   344→Changed apps/api/app/schemas/**   →  bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   345→Changed apps/api/app/models/**    →  bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "reason"
   346→Need a new package                →  bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh <lang> <pkg>
   347→Need the local stack running      →  bash skills/docker/run.sh up
   348→Feature complete                  →  bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "description"
   349→Want to remember something        →  bash skills/memory/run.sh save "content"
   350→```
   351→
   352→---
   353→
   354→## Agent Memory — Local Vector Database
   355→
   356→### Why Agents Need Memory
   357→
   358→An AI agent without memory is goldfish-like — every session, it starts from zero. It re-asks questions it has already answered, forgets user preferences it learned last week, re-investigates bugs it already traced.
   359→
   360→Memory changes this. An agent can:
   361→- Record a decision: *"We decided to use UUIDs for all primary keys"*
   362→- Record a user preference: *"User always wants dark mode defaults"*
   363→- Record a debugging discovery: *"The auth bug was caused by a missing await on the token refresh"*
   364→- ...and recall any of this semantically in a future session
   365→
   366→### How It Works Under the Hood
   367→
   368→Memory uses **ChromaDB**, a local vector database that runs entirely in the repo directory. No external API. No account needed. No data leaves your machine.
   369→
   370→When you save a memory:
   371→1. ChromaDB **embeds** your text into a high-dimensional vector using a local embedding model
   372→2. It stores the vector alongside the original text and any metadata in `.data/chromadb/`
   373→3. The data persists on disk across sessions
   374→
   375→When you search:
   376→1. Your query gets embedded into the same vector space
   377→2. ChromaDB finds the **nearest vectors** using cosine similarity
   378→3. Returns results sorted by relevance score (0.0–1.0)
   379→
   380→This is **semantic search** — not keyword matching. You can search for *"user UI preferences"* and find a memory that says *"the product owner wants a minimal, light interface"*, even though none of those words overlap.
   381→
   382→```
   383→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   384→│                    ChromaDB Vector Store                         │
   385→│                                                                  │
   386→│  Memory: "User prefers dark mode"                               │
   387→│  Vector: [0.23, -0.87, 0.41, 0.12, ...]  (768 dimensions)      │
   388→│  Tags:   ["user_42", "preferences"]                             │
   389→│  Time:   2026-02-18T10:30:00                                    │
   390→│                                                                  │
   391→│  Memory: "Auth bug caused by missing await on token refresh"    │
   392→│  Vector: [0.11, 0.34, -0.62, 0.88, ...]                        │
   393→│  Tags:   ["bugs", "auth"]                                       │
   394→│                                                                  │
   395→│  Search: "why did auth break?"                                  │
   396→│  → cosine similarity against all vectors                        │
   397→│  → returns [auth bug memory, score: 0.92]                       │
   398→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   399→```
   400→
   401→### Using Memory As a Developer
   402→
   403→The memory service is importable directly in Python:
   404→
   405→```python
   406→from app.services.memory import MemoryService
   407→
   408→svc = MemoryService()
   409→
   410→# Save something
   411→svc.add(
   412→    "We use UUIDs for all primary keys across the API",
   413→    tags=["architecture", "decisions"],
   414→    metadata={"decided_by": "team", "date": "2026-02-18"},
   415→)
   416→
   417→# Recall semantically
   418→results = svc.search("what type of IDs do we use?")
   419→for r in results:
   420→    print(f"[{r['score']:.0%}] {r['content']}")
   421→
   422→# List by tag
   423→user_prefs = svc.list_memories(tags=["user_42"])
   424→
   425→# Delete
   426→svc.delete(memory_id="abc-123")
   427→```
   428→
   429→### Using Memory As an Agent
   430→
   431→Agents interact via the `memory` skill from anywhere in the repo:
   432→
   433→```bash
   434→# Save a memory (with optional comma-separated tags)
   435→bash skills/memory/run.sh save "The user wants pagination on all list endpoints" "api,ux"
   436→
   437→# Recall memories semantically
   438→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "what did we decide about lists?"
   439→
   440→# List all memories tagged with a specific tag
   441→bash skills/memory/run.sh list "api"
   442→```
   443→
   444→**Example output from `recall`:**
   445→
   446→```
   447→=== Recalling memories ===
   448→Found 2 memories:
   449→
   450→[94% match] The user wants pagination on all list endpoints
   451→  ID: 7f3c1a2b | Tags: api,ux
   452→
   453→[71% match] List endpoints should return total count alongside results
   454→  ID: 9e2b8d4f | Tags: api
   455→```
   456→
   457→### Memory API Endpoints
   458→
   459→The memory system is also exposed over HTTP for any service or tool to use:
   460→
   461→```
   462→POST /memory/add
   463→Body: { "content": "string", "tags": ["string"], "metadata": {} }
   464→
   465→POST /memory/search
   466→Body: { "query": "string", "tags": ["string"], "limit": 10 }
   467→```
   468→
   469→---
   470→
   471→## Multi-Agent Coordination
   472→
   473→Multiple AI agents can work in this repo simultaneously. Without rules, they conflict. Here is the system:
   474→
   475→### Zone Ownership
   476→
   477→Each agent is assigned a zone. Within a zone, an agent operates freely. Crossing zones requires care.
   478→
   479→| Zone | Path | What lives here |
   480→|------|------|-----------------|
   481→| Backend | `apps/api/` | FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, migrations |
   482→| Frontend | `apps/web/` | Next.js pages, components, hooks |
   483→| Shared Types | `packages/shared-types/` | Auto-generated — owned by `type-sync` |
   484→| UI Library | `packages/ui/` | Shared React components |
   485→| Skills | `skills/` | Skill scripts |
   486→
   487→### The Type Contract
   488→
   489→`openapi.json` and `packages/shared-types/src/api-types.ts` are **auto-generated files**. Do not edit them manually. They are owned by the `type-sync` skill.
   490→
   491→The contract is simple:
   492→- Backend agent changes a Pydantic schema → runs `type-sync`
   493→- `type-sync` regenerates `openapi.json` and `api-types.ts`
   494→- Frontend agent imports from `@repo/shared-types` — types are always up to date
   495→
   496→### Commit Conventions
   497→
   498→Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) scoped to zones:
   499→
   500→```
   501→feat(api): add user authentication endpoint
   502→fix(web): correct pagination on search results
   503→chore(deps): bump fastapi to 0.116.0
   504→docs(skills): update memory skill README
   505→refactor(api): extract email validation to service layer
   506→checkpoint: auth feature complete
   507→```
   508→
   509→---
   510→
   511→## Context Versioning with Entire
   512→
   513→[Entire](https://entire.dev) is a CLI that hooks into git and snapshots an AI agent's reasoning alongside every commit. This means you can look back at *why* a change was made, not just *what* changed.
   514→
   515→The `checkpoint` skill wraps this:
   516→
   517→```bash
   518→# Creates a git commit + Entire context snapshot
   519→bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "finished user auth"
   520→```
   521→
   522→**Browse history:**
   523→```bash
   524→entire log              # List all checkpoints
   525→entire diff <id>        # See what changed between checkpoints
   526→```
   527→
   528→---
   529→
   530→## Backend Deep Dive
   531→
   532→### Configuration
   533→
   534→All configuration lives in `app/core/config.py` using **Pydantic Settings**. It reads from environment variables and `.env` files automatically.
   535→
   536→```python
   537→from app.core.config import settings
   538→
   539→print(settings.DATABASE_URL)
   540→print(settings.MEMORY_DB_PATH)
   541→print(settings.DEBUG)
   542→```
   543→
   544→Never hardcode config values. Never use `os.environ.get()` directly. Always go through `settings`.
   545→
   546→### Structured Logging
   547→
   548→The logging system produces human-readable colored output in development and machine-parseable JSON in production.
   549→
   550→```python
   551→from app.core.logging import get_logger
   552→
   553→logger = get_logger(__name__)
   554→
   555→# Basic
   556→logger.info("user signed up")
   557→
   558→# With structured fields (show up as JSON keys in production)
   559→logger.info("payment processed", user_id=42, amount_cents=1999, currency="USD")
   560→
   561→# Warnings and errors
   562→logger.warning("rate limit approaching", requests_remaining=5)
   563→logger.error("payment failed", order_id="abc", exc_info=True)
   564→```
   565→
   566→**Never use `print()`**. It bypasses log levels and structured output.
   567→
   568→Switch to JSON output in production by setting `LOG_JSON=true`.
   569→
   570→### Database Layer
   571→
   572→SQLAlchemy 2.0 with synchronous sessions (async can be added if needed).
   573→
   574→**Defining a model:**
   575→
   576→```python
   577→# apps/api/app/models/user.py
   578→from sqlalchemy import String
   579→from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column
   580→from app.core.database import Base
   581→
   582→class User(Base):
   583→    __tablename__ = "users"
   584→
   585→    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
   586→    email: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), unique=True)
   587→    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100))
   588→```
   589→
   590→**Import it** in `app/models/__init__.py` so Alembic can discover it:
   591→
   592→```python
   593→from app.models.user import User  # noqa: F401
   594→```
   595→
   596→**Create the migration:**
   597→
   598→```bash
   599→bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "add users table"
   600→```
   601→
   602→**Using the DB in routes:**
   603→
   604→```python
   605→from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
   606→from fastapi import Depends
   607→from app.core.database import get_db
   608→
   609→@router.get("/users")
   610→def list_users(db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
   611→    return db.query(User).all()
   612→```
   613→
   614→### Adding an Endpoint
   615→
   616→Here is the full workflow for a new endpoint:
   617→
   618→**1. Define the Pydantic schemas** in `app/schemas/`:
   619→
   620→```python
   621→# app/schemas/user.py
   622→from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr
   623→
   624→class UserCreateRequest(BaseModel):
   625→    email: str
   626→    name: str
   627→
   628→class UserResponse(BaseModel):
   629→    id: int
   630→    email: str
   631→    name: str
   632→```
   633→
   634→**2. Add them to** `app/schemas/__init__.py`:
   635→
   636→```python
   637→from app.schemas.user import UserCreateRequest, UserResponse
   638→__all__ = [..., "UserCreateRequest", "UserResponse"]
   639→```
   640→
   641→**3. Write the route** in `app/api/routes/users.py`:
   642→
   643→```python
   644→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
   645→from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
   646→from app.core.database import get_db
   647→from app.core.logging import get_logger
   648→from app.schemas.user import UserCreateRequest, UserResponse
   649→from app.models.user import User
   650→
   651→logger = get_logger(__name__)
   652→router = APIRouter(prefix="/users")
   653→
   654→@router.post("/", response_model=UserResponse)
   655→def create_user(req: UserCreateRequest, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
   656→    user = User(email=req.email, name=req.name)
   657→    db.add(user)
   658→    db.commit()
   659→    logger.info("user created", email=req.email)
   660→    return user
   661→```
   662→
   663→**4. Register the router** in `app/api/routes/__init__.py`:
   664→
   665→```python
   666→from app.api.routes import health, memory, users
   667→
   668→api_router.include_router(users.router, tags=["users"])
   669→```
   670→
   671→**5. Sync the types:**
   672→
   673→```bash
   674→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   675→```
   676→
   677→Done. The frontend now has TypeScript types for `UserCreateRequest` and `UserResponse`.
   678→
   679→---
   680→
   681→## Frontend Deep Dive
   682→
   683→The frontend follows Next.js 15+ App Router conventions.
   684→
   685→**Use types from shared package:**
   686→
   687→```typescript
   688→import type { HealthResponse } from "@repo/shared-types";
   689→
   690→async function fetchHealth(): Promise<HealthResponse> {
   691→  const res = await fetch("http://localhost:8000/health");
   692→  return res.json();
   693→}
   694→```
   695→
   696→**Use components from UI library:**
   697→
   698→```typescript
   699→import { Button } from "@repo/ui";
   700→
   701→export default function Page() {
   702→  return <Button variant="primary">Click me</Button>;
   703→}
   704→```
   705→
   706→**Pages go in** `src/app/` following App Router conventions:
   707→
   708→```
   709→src/app/
   710→├── layout.tsx          # Root layout (html, body, providers)
   711→├── page.tsx            # Home page (/)
   712→├── users/
   713→│   ├── page.tsx        # /users
   714→│   └── [id]/page.tsx   # /users/:id
   715→```
   716→
   717→---
   718→
   719→## Development Workflow
   720→
   721→### Daily workflow for a feature
   722→
   723→```bash
   724→# 1. Start dev servers
   725→pnpm dev
   726→
   727→# 2. Make changes to backend schema
   728→# edit apps/api/app/schemas/user.py
   729→
   730→# 3. Sync types to frontend automatically
   731→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   732→
   733→# 4. Add a new package if needed
   734→bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh py httpx
   735→bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh js zod web
   736→
   737→# 5. Save important decisions to agent memory
   738→bash skills/memory/run.sh save "User endpoints use cursor-based pagination" "api,decisions"
   739→
   740→# 6. Fix linting
   741→bash skills/lint-fix/run.sh
   742→
   743→# 7. Run tests
   744→bash skills/test-run/run.sh
   745→
   746→# 8. Commit and checkpoint
   747→bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "add user endpoints with pagination"
   748→```
   749→
   750→### Adding a migration
   751→
   752→```bash
   753→# After modifying any file in apps/api/app/models/
   754→bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "add email_verified column to users"
   755→```
   756→
   757→### Recalling past decisions
   758→
   759→```bash
   760→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "pagination approach"
   761→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "why we use UUIDs"
   762→bash skills/memory/run.sh list "decisions"
   763→```
   764→
   765→---
   766→
   767→## Docker
   768→
   769→The monorepo ships with a production-ready Docker setup. One command starts the entire stack.
   770→
   771→### Services
   772→
   773→| Service | Image | Port | Volume |
   774→|---------|-------|------|--------|
   775→| `api` | Built from `apps/api/Dockerfile` | 8000 | `chromadb_data:/app/.data/chromadb` |
   776→| `postgres` | `postgres:16-alpine` | 5432 | `postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data` |
   777→
   778→Both services have health checks. The API waits for Postgres to be healthy before starting.
   779→
   780→### Docker Commands
   781→
   782→```bash
   783→# Start everything (builds images if needed)
   784→bash skills/docker/run.sh up
   785→
   786→# Run Alembic migrations inside the API container
   787→bash skills/docker/run.sh migrate
   788→
   789→# Tail all logs
   790→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs
   791→
   792→# Tail logs for one service
   793→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs api
   794→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs postgres
   795→
   796→# Stop containers (data volumes preserved)
   797→bash skills/docker/run.sh down
   798→
   799→# Rebuild images without starting
   800→bash skills/docker/run.sh build
   801→
   802→# Open an interactive shell inside the API container
   803→bash skills/docker/run.sh shell
   804→
   805→# ⚠ Destroy everything including volumes (all data gone)
   806→bash skills/docker/run.sh reset
   807→```
   808→
   809→### Connecting to Postgres
   810→
   811→**Via Docker:**
   812→```bash
   813→docker compose exec postgres psql -U monorepo -d monorepo
   814→```
   815→
   816→**From a local client (port 5432 is exposed):**
   817→```
   818→postgresql://monorepo:monorepo@localhost:5432/monorepo
   819→```
   820→
   821→To use a different database name or credentials, copy `.env.example` → `.env` and edit:
   822→```bash
   823→cp .env.example .env
   824→# Then edit POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DB
   825→```
   826→
   827→---
   828→
   829→## Environment Variables Reference
   830→
   831→**`apps/api/.env`** — for running the backend locally (copy from `apps/api/.env.example`):
   832→
   833→| Variable | Default | Description |
   834→|----------|---------|-------------|
   835→| `APP_NAME` | `AI-Native Monorepo API` | Displayed in API docs |
   836→| `APP_VERSION` | `0.1.0` | Displayed in API docs |
   837→| `DEBUG` | `false` | Enables SQLAlchemy query logging |
   838→| `DATABASE_URL` | `sqlite:///./dev.db` | SQLAlchemy connection string |
   839→| `MEMORY_DB_PATH` | `.data/chromadb` | Path for ChromaDB vector store |
   840→| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | Logging threshold (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR) |
   841→| `LOG_JSON` | `false` | Set `true` in production for JSON log output |
   842→
   843→**`.env`** — for Docker Compose (copy from `.env.example` at the repo root):
   844→
   845→| Variable | Default | Description |
   846→|----------|---------|-------------|
   847→| `POSTGRES_USER` | `monorepo` | Postgres username |
   848→| `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` | `monorepo` | Postgres password |
   849→| `POSTGRES_DB` | `monorepo` | Postgres database name |
   850→| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | API log level inside Docker |
   851→| `DEBUG` | `false` | API debug mode inside Docker |
   852→
   853→For production `DATABASE_URL`:
   854→```
   855→DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg2://user:pass@host:5432/dbname
   856→```
   857→

1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: ["**"]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→    branches: ["**"]
     8→
     9→concurrency:
    10→  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
    11→  cancel-in-progress: true
    12→
    13→jobs:
    14→  # ── Detect which parts of the repo changed ──────────────────────────────────
    15→  changes:
    16→    name: Detect changes
    17→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    18→    outputs:
    19→      backend:  ${{ steps.filter.outputs.backend }}
    20→      frontend: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.frontend }}
    21→      shared:   ${{ steps.filter.outputs.shared }}
    22→    steps:
    23→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    24→      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
    25→        id: filter
    26→        with:
    27→          filters: |
    28→            backend:
    29→              - 'apps/api/**'
    30→              - 'pyproject.toml'
    31→              - 'uv.lock'
    32→            frontend:
    33→              - 'apps/web/**'
    34→              - 'packages/ui/**'
    35→            shared:
    36→              - 'packages/shared-types/**'
    37→
    38→  # ── Lint & type-check ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    39→  lint:
    40→    name: Lint (${{ matrix.runner }})
    41→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    42→    needs: changes
    43→    if: |
    44→      needs.changes.outputs.backend == 'true' ||
    45→      needs.changes.outputs.frontend == 'true' ||
    46→      needs.changes.outputs.shared == 'true'
    47→    strategy:
    48→      fail-fast: false
    49→      matrix:
    50→        runner: [python, node]
    51→
    52→    steps:
    53→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    54→
    55→      # ── Python linting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→      - name: Set up Python
    57→        if: matrix.runner == 'python'
    58→        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
    59→        with:
    60→          python-version: "3.11"
    61→
    62→      - name: Install uv
    63→        if: matrix.runner == 'python'
    64→        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4
    65→        with:
    66→          enable-cache: true
    67→          cache-dependency-glob: "uv.lock"
    68→
    69→      - name: Install Python dev dependencies
    70→        if: matrix.runner == 'python'
    71→        run: uv sync --dev
    72→
    73→      - name: Ruff lint
    74→        if: matrix.runner == 'python'
    75→        run: uv run ruff check apps/api/app/
    76→
    77→      - name: Ruff format check
    78→        if: matrix.runner == 'python'
    79→        run: uv run ruff format --check apps/api/app/
    80→
    81→      # ── Node linting ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    82→      - name: Set up Node
    83→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
    84→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    85→        with:
    86→          node-version: "20"
    87→
    88→      - name: Set up pnpm
    89→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
    90→        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
    91→        with:
    92→          version: "10.30.0"
    93→          run_install: false
    94→
    95→      - name: Get pnpm store path
    96→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
    97→        id: pnpm-cache
    98→        run: echo "store=$(pnpm store path --silent)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    99→
   100→      - name: Cache pnpm store
   101→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
   102→        uses: actions/cache@v4
   103→        with:
   104→          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-cache.outputs.store }}
   105→          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
   106→          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-
   107→
   108→      - name: Install JS dependencies
   109→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
   110→        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   111→
   112→      - name: Turbo lint
   113→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
   114→        run: pnpm turbo run lint
   115→
   116→      - name: TypeScript type-check (web)
   117→        if: matrix.runner == 'node'
   118→        run: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --project apps/web/tsconfig.json
   119→        env:
   120→          NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://localhost:8000
   121→
   122→  # ── Python tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   123→  test-api:
   124→    name: Test API
   125→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   126→    needs: changes
   127→    if: needs.changes.outputs.backend == 'true'
   128→
   129→    services:
   130→      postgres:
   131→        image: postgres:16-alpine
   132→        env:
   133→          POSTGRES_USER: test
   134→          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test
   135→          POSTGRES_DB: test_db
   136→        ports:
   137→          - 5432:5432
   138→        options: >-
   139→          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U test"
   140→          --health-interval 5s
   141→          --health-timeout 5s
   142→          --health-retries 10
   143→
   144→    steps:
   145→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   146→
   147→      - name: Set up Python
   148→        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
   149→        with:
   150→          python-version: "3.11"
   151→
   152→      - name: Install uv
   153→        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4
   154→        with:
   155→          enable-cache: true
   156→          cache-dependency-glob: "uv.lock"
   157→
   158→      - name: Install Python dev dependencies
   159→        run: uv sync --dev
   160→
   161→      - name: Run Alembic migrations
   162→        working-directory: apps/api
   163→        env:
   164→          DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://test:test@localhost:5432/test_db
   165→        run: uv run alembic upgrade head
   166→
   167→      - name: Run pytest
   168→        working-directory: apps/api
   169→        env:
   170→          DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://test:test@localhost:5432/test_db
   171→          MEMORY_DB_PATH: /tmp/test-chromadb
   172→          LOG_JSON: "false"
   173→          LOG_LEVEL: WARNING
   174→        run: uv run pytest -v --tb=short
   175→
   176→  # ── Next.js build check ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   177→  test-web:
   178→    name: Test Web (build)
   179→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   180→    needs: changes
   181→    if: |
   182→      needs.changes.outputs.frontend == 'true' ||
   183→      needs.changes.outputs.shared == 'true'
   184→
   185→    steps:
   186→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   187→
   188→      - name: Set up Node
   189→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   190→        with:
   191→          node-version: "20"
   192→
   193→      - name: Set up pnpm
   194→        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
   195→        with:
   196→          version: "10.30.0"
   197→          run_install: false
   198→
   199→      - name: Get pnpm store path
   200→        id: pnpm-cache
   201→        run: echo "store=$(pnpm store path --silent)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   202→
   203→      - name: Cache pnpm store
   204→        uses: actions/cache@v4
   205→        with:
   206→          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-cache.outputs.store }}
   207→          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
   208→          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-
   209→
   210→      - name: Cache Turbo
   211→        uses: actions/cache@v4
   212→        with:
   213→          path: .turbo
   214→          key: turbo-${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.sha }}
   215→          restore-keys: turbo-${{ runner.os }}-
   216→
   217→      - name: Install JS dependencies
   218→        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   219→
   220→      - name: Build shared packages first
   221→        run: pnpm turbo run build --filter=@repo/shared-types --filter=@repo/ui
   222→
   223→      - name: Build web
   224→        run: pnpm turbo run build --filter=web
   225→        env:
   226→          NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://placeholder.example.com
   227→          NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
   228→
   229→  # ── Full build gate ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   230→  build-check:
   231→    name: Full build check
   232→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   233→    needs: [lint, test-api, test-web]
   234→    # Run if at least one upstream job ran (and none failed)
   235→    if: |
   236→      always() &&
   237→      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&
   238→      !contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled')
   239→
   240→    steps:
   241→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   242→
   243→      - name: Set up Node
   244→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   245→        with:
   246→          node-version: "20"
   247→
   248→      - name: Set up pnpm
   249→        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
   250→        with:
   251→          version: "10.30.0"
   252→          run_install: false
   253→
   254→      - name: Get pnpm store path
   255→        id: pnpm-cache
   256→        run: echo "store=$(pnpm store path --silent)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   257→
   258→      - name: Cache pnpm store
   259→        uses: actions/cache@v4
   260→        with:
   261→          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-cache.outputs.store }}
   262→          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
   263→          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-
   264→
   265→      - name: Cache Turbo
   266→        uses: actions/cache@v4
   267→        with:
   268→          path: .turbo
   269→          key: turbo-${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.sha }}
   270→          restore-keys: turbo-${{ runner.os }}-
   271→
   272→      - name: Install JS dependencies
   273→        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   274→
   275→      - name: Turbo full build
   276→        run: pnpm turbo run build
   277→        env:
   278→          NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://placeholder.example.com
   279→          NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
   280→

1→name: Deploy
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→
     7→# Never cancel an in-flight deploy — always let it finish.
     8→concurrency:
     9→  group: deploy-production
    10→  cancel-in-progress: false
    11→
    12→env:
    13→  GCP_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}
    14→  GCP_REGION:     ${{ vars.GCP_REGION || 'us-central1' }}
    15→  GAR_LOCATION:   ${{ vars.GAR_LOCATION || 'us-central1' }}
    16→  GAR_REPO:       ${{ vars.GAR_REPO || 'monorepo' }}
    17→
    18→jobs:
    19→  # ── Deploy API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→  deploy-api:
    21→    name: Deploy API → Cloud Run
    22→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    23→    environment: production
    24→    permissions:
    25→      contents: read
    26→      id-token: write   # Required for Workload Identity Federation
    27→
    28→    steps:
    29→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    30→
    31→      - name: Authenticate to GCP (Workload Identity Federation)
    32→        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
    33→        with:
    34→          workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.WIF_PROVIDER }}
    35→          service_account: ${{ secrets.WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}
    36→
    37→      - name: Set up gcloud CLI
    38→        uses: google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2
    39→
    40→      - name: Configure Docker auth for Artifact Registry
    41→        run: gcloud auth configure-docker ${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev --quiet
    42→
    43→      - name: Set image tag
    44→        id: tag
    45→        run: |
    46→          SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
    47→          SHORT="${SHA:0:7}"
    48→          BASE="${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev/${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}/${{ env.GAR_REPO }}/api"
    49→          echo "image=${BASE}:${SHORT}"       >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    50→          echo "latest=${BASE}:latest"         >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    51→          echo "short_sha=${SHORT}"            >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    52→
    53→      - name: Build API Docker image
    54→        run: |
    55→          docker build \
    56→            -f apps/api/Dockerfile \
    57→            -t ${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }} \
    58→            -t ${{ steps.tag.outputs.latest }} \
    59→            .
    60→
    61→      - name: Push API image to Artifact Registry
    62→        run: |
    63→          docker push ${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }}
    64→          docker push ${{ steps.tag.outputs.latest }}
    65→
    66→      - name: Deploy API to Cloud Run
    67→        run: |
    68→          gcloud run deploy api \
    69→            --image "${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }}" \
    70→            --region "${{ env.GCP_REGION }}" \
    71→            --project "${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}" \
    72→            --platform managed \
    73→            --set-secrets "DATABASE_URL=DATABASE_URL:latest" \
    74→            --set-env-vars "LOG_JSON=true,LOG_LEVEL=INFO,DEBUG=false,REDACTED" \
    75→            --min-instances 1 \
    76→            --max-instances 10 \
    77→            --memory 1Gi \
    78→            --cpu 1 \
    79→            --timeout 300 \
    80→            --concurrency 40
    81→
    82→      - name: Get API URL
    83→        id: api-url
    84→        run: |
    85→          URL="$(gcloud run services describe api \
    86→            --region "${{ env.GCP_REGION }}" \
    87→            --project "${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}" \
    88→            --format 'value(status.url)')"
    89→          echo "url=$URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    90→          echo "API deployed: $URL"
    91→
    92→      - name: Smoke test API health endpoint
    93→        run: |
    94→          sleep 5
    95→          STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    96→            "${{ steps.api-url.outputs.url }}/health" || echo "000")
    97→          echo "Health check status: $STATUS"
    98→          if [[ "$STATUS" != "200" ]]; then
    99→            echo "ERROR: API health check failed with status $STATUS"
   100→            exit 1
   101→          fi
   102→          echo "✓ API is healthy"
   103→
   104→  # ── Deploy Web ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   105→  deploy-web:
   106→    name: Deploy Web → Cloud Run
   107→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   108→    environment: production
   109→    needs: [deploy-api]
   110→    permissions:
   111→      contents: read
   112→      id-token: write
   113→
   114→    steps:
   115→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   116→
   117→      - name: Authenticate to GCP (Workload Identity Federation)
   118→        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
   119→        with:
   120→          workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.WIF_PROVIDER }}
   121→          service_account: ${{ secrets.WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}
   122→
   123→      - name: Set up gcloud CLI
   124→        uses: google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2
   125→
   126→      - name: Configure Docker auth for Artifact Registry
   127→        run: gcloud auth configure-docker ${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev --quiet
   128→
   129→      - name: Resolve production API URL
   130→        id: api-url
   131→        run: |
   132→          # Prefer the GitHub secret (set it after first API deploy).
   133→          # Falls back to auto-detecting from Cloud Run.
   134→          PROD_API_URL="${{ secrets.PROD_API_URL }}"
   135→          if [[ -z "$PROD_API_URL" ]]; then
   136→            PROD_API_URL="$(gcloud run services describe api \
   137→              --region "${{ env.GCP_REGION }}" \
   138→              --project "${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}" \
   139→              --format 'value(status.url)')"
   140→          fi
   141→          echo "url=$PROD_API_URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   142→          echo "Building web with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=$PROD_API_URL"
   143→
   144→      - name: Set image tag
   145→        id: tag
   146→        run: |
   147→          SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
   148→          SHORT="${SHA:0:7}"
   149→          BASE="${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev/${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}/${{ env.GAR_REPO }}/web"
   150→          echo "image=${BASE}:${SHORT}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   151→          echo "latest=${BASE}:latest"  >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   152→
   153→      - name: Build Web Docker image
   154→        run: |
   155→          docker build \
   156→            -f apps/web/Dockerfile \
   157→            --build-arg "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=${{ steps.api-url.outputs.url }}" \
   158→            -t ${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }} \
   159→            -t ${{ steps.tag.outputs.latest }} \
   160→            .
   161→        env:
   162→          NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
   163→
   164→      - name: Push Web image to Artifact Registry
   165→        run: |
   166→          docker push ${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }}
   167→          docker push ${{ steps.tag.outputs.latest }}
   168→
   169→      - name: Deploy Web to Cloud Run
   170→        run: |
   171→          gcloud run deploy web \
   172→            --image "${{ steps.tag.outputs.image }}" \
   173→            --region "${{ env.GCP_REGION }}" \
   174→            --project "${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}" \
   175→            --platform managed \
   176→            --allow-unauthenticated \
   177→            --min-instances 0 \
   178→            --max-instances 5 \
   179→            --memory 512Mi \
   180→            --cpu 1 \
   181→            --timeout 60 \
   182→            --concurrency 80
   183→
   184→      - name: Get Web URL
   185→        run: |
   186→          URL="$(gcloud run services describe web \
   187→            --region "${{ env.GCP_REGION }}" \
   188→            --project "${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}" \
   189→            --format 'value(status.url)')"
   190→          echo "Web deployed: $URL"
   191→

1→name: PR
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  pull_request:
     5→    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled, unlabeled]
     6→
     7→permissions:
     8→  contents: read
     9→  pull-requests: write
    10→
    11→jobs:
    12→  # ── Auto-label PRs based on changed paths ───────────────────────────────────
    13→  label:
    14→    name: Label PR
    15→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16→    steps:
    17→      - uses: actions/labeler@v5
    18→        with:
    19→          repo-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    20→          configuration-path: .github/labeler.yml
    21→
    22→  # ── Build only affected packages ────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→  affected:
    24→    name: Build affected packages
    25→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    26→
    27→    steps:
    28→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    29→        with:
    30→          fetch-depth: 0   # Full history needed for turbo --filter comparison
    31→
    32→      - name: Set up Node
    33→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    34→        with:
    35→          node-version: "20"
    36→
    37→      - name: Set up pnpm
    38→        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
    39→        with:
    40→          version: "10.30.0"
    41→          run_install: false
    42→
    43→      - name: Get pnpm store path
    44→        id: pnpm-cache
    45→        run: echo "store=$(pnpm store path --silent)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    46→
    47→      - name: Cache pnpm store
    48→        uses: actions/cache@v4
    49→        with:
    50→          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-cache.outputs.store }}
    51→          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
    52→          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-
    53→
    54→      - name: Cache Turbo
    55→        uses: actions/cache@v4
    56→        with:
    57→          path: .turbo
    58→          key: turbo-pr-${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.sha }}
    59→          restore-keys: turbo-pr-${{ runner.os }}-
    60→
    61→      - name: Install JS dependencies
    62→        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
    63→
    64→      - name: Build and lint affected packages
    65→        run: |
    66→          pnpm turbo run build lint --filter=...[origin/${{ github.base_ref }}]
    67→        env:
    68→          NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: http://placeholder.example.com
    69→          NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
    70→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
     5→cd "$REPO_ROOT"
     6→
     7→TARGET="${1:-help}"
     8→
     9→# ── Configuration (override via environment variables) ────────────────────────
    10→GCP_PROJECT="${GCP_PROJECT:-$(gcloud config get-value project 2>/dev/null || true)}"
    11→GCP_REGION="${GCP_REGION:-us-central1}"
    12→GAR_LOCATION="${GAR_LOCATION:-us-central1}"
    13→GAR_REPO="${GAR_REPO:-monorepo}"
    14→API_SERVICE="${API_SERVICE:-api}"
    15→WEB_SERVICE="${WEB_SERVICE:-web}"
    16→IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)}"
    17→
    18→GAR_BASE="${GAR_LOCATION}-docker.pkg.dev/${GCP_PROJECT}/${GAR_REPO}"
    19→
    20→function check_prereqs() {
    21→    if ! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
    22→        echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker CLI."
    23→        exit 1
    24→    fi
    25→    if ! command -v gcloud &> /dev/null; then
    26→        echo "ERROR: gcloud not found. Install Google Cloud SDK."
    27→        exit 1
    28→    fi
    29→    if [[ -z "$GCP_PROJECT" ]]; then
    30→        echo "ERROR: GCP_PROJECT not set and gcloud config has no project."
    31→        echo "  Run: gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID"
    32→        echo "  Or:  GCP_PROJECT=my-project bash skills/deploy/run.sh api"
    33→        exit 1
    34→    fi
    35→}
    36→
    37→function show_help() {
    38→    cat << EOF
    39→=== Deploy Skill ===
    40→
    41→Deploy API or Web service to Google Cloud Run.
    42→
    43→Usage:
    44→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh api                  # Deploy API
    45→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh web                  # Deploy Web (auto-detects API URL)
    46→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh all                  # Deploy API then Web
    47→
    48→Environment overrides:
    49→  GCP_PROJECT     GCP project ID (default: gcloud config)
    50→  GCP_REGION      Cloud Run region (default: us-central1)
    51→  GAR_LOCATION    Artifact Registry location (default: us-central1)
    52→  GAR_REPO        Artifact Registry repo name (default: monorepo)
    53→  IMAGE_TAG       Docker image tag (default: git SHA short)
    54→  PROD_API_URL    API URL baked into web image (default: auto-detect from Cloud Run)
    55→
    56→Prerequisites:
    57→  gcloud auth login
    58→  gcloud auth configure-docker ${GAR_LOCATION}-docker.pkg.dev
    59→  gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID
    60→
    61→WARNING: For production, prefer pushing to main to trigger the GitHub Actions
    62→         deploy workflow. Use this skill for emergency deploys only.
    63→EOF
    64→}
    65→
    66→function deploy_api() {
    67→    local api_image="${GAR_BASE}/${API_SERVICE}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    68→    local api_latest="${GAR_BASE}/${API_SERVICE}:latest"
    69→
    70→    echo ""
    71→    echo "=== deploy/api: Building image ==="
    72→    echo "  Image: $api_image"
    73→    docker build \
    74→        -f apps/api/Dockerfile \
    75→        -t "$api_image" \
    76→        -t "$api_latest" \
    77→        .
    78→
    79→    echo ""
    80→    echo "=== deploy/api: Pushing image ==="
    81→    docker push "$api_image"
    82→    docker push "$api_latest"
    83→
    84→    echo ""
    85→    echo "=== deploy/api: Deploying to Cloud Run ==="
    86→    gcloud run deploy "$API_SERVICE" \
    87→        --image "$api_image" \
    88→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
    89→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
    90→        --platform managed \
    91→        --set-secrets "DATABASE_URL=DATABASE_URL:latest" \
    92→        --set-env-vars "LOG_JSON=true,LOG_LEVEL=INFO,DEBUG=false,REDACTED" \
    93→        --min-instances 1 \
    94→        --max-instances 10 \
    95→        --memory 1Gi \
    96→        --cpu 1 \
    97→        --timeout 300 \
    98→        --concurrency 40
    99→
   100→    echo ""
   101→    echo "=== deploy/api: Done ==="
   102→    local url
   103→    url="$(gcloud run services describe "$API_SERVICE" \
   104→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   105→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   106→        --format "value(status.url)")"
   107→    echo "  URL: $url"
   108→}
   109→
   110→function deploy_web() {
   111→    # NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL must be baked into the image at build time.
   112→    # Auto-detect from the deployed API service if not provided.
   113→    local prod_api_url="${PROD_API_URL:-}"
   114→    if [[ -z "$prod_api_url" ]]; then
   115→        echo "=== deploy/web: Auto-detecting API URL from Cloud Run ==="
   116→        prod_api_url="$(gcloud run services describe "$API_SERVICE" \
   117→            --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   118→            --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   119→            --format "value(status.url)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
   120→        if [[ -z "$prod_api_url" ]]; then
   121→            echo "ERROR: Could not detect API URL. Deploy the API first, or set PROD_API_URL."
   122→            exit 1
   123→        fi
   124→        echo "  Detected: $prod_api_url"
   125→    fi
   126→
   127→    local web_image="${GAR_BASE}/${WEB_SERVICE}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
   128→    local web_latest="${GAR_BASE}/${WEB_SERVICE}:latest"
   129→
   130→    echo ""
   131→    echo "=== deploy/web: Building image ==="
   132→    echo "  Image: $web_image"
   133→    echo "  NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: $prod_api_url"
   134→    docker build \
   135→        -f apps/web/Dockerfile \
   136→        --build-arg "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=${prod_api_url}" \
   137→        -t "$web_image" \
   138→        -t "$web_latest" \
   139→        .
   140→
   141→    echo ""
   142→    echo "=== deploy/web: Pushing image ==="
   143→    docker push "$web_image"
   144→    docker push "$web_latest"
   145→
   146→    echo ""
   147→    echo "=== deploy/web: Deploying to Cloud Run ==="
   148→    gcloud run deploy "$WEB_SERVICE" \
   149→        --image "$web_image" \
   150→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   151→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   152→        --platform managed \
   153→        --allow-unauthenticated \
   154→        --min-instances 0 \
   155→        --max-instances 5 \
   156→        --memory 512Mi \
   157→        --cpu 1 \
   158→        --timeout 60 \
   159→        --concurrency 80
   160→
   161→    echo ""
   162→    echo "=== deploy/web: Done ==="
   163→    local url
   164→    url="$(gcloud run services describe "$WEB_SERVICE" \
   165→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   166→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   167→        --format "value(status.url)")"
   168→    echo "  URL: $url"
   169→}
   170→
   171→case "$TARGET" in
   172→    api)
   173→        check_prereqs
   174→        deploy_api
   175→        ;;
   176→    web)
   177→        check_prereqs
   178→        deploy_web
   179→        ;;
   180→    all)
   181→        check_prereqs
   182→        deploy_api
   183→        deploy_web
   184→        ;;
   185→    help|--help|-h)
   186→        show_help
   187→        ;;
   188→    *)
   189→        echo "Error: Unknown target '$TARGET'"
   190→        echo ""
   191→        show_help
   192→        exit 1
   193→        ;;
   194→esac
   195→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
     5→cd "$REPO_ROOT"
     6→
     7→ACTION="${1:-help}"
     8→
     9→function show_help() {
    10→    cat << 'EOF'
    11→=== Docker Skill ===
    12→
    13→Manage the full monorepo stack (API + PostgreSQL) via Docker Compose.
    14→
    15→Usage:
    16→  bash skills/docker/run.sh up        # Build images and start all services
    17→  bash skills/docker/run.sh down      # Stop and remove containers
    18→  bash skills/docker/run.sh restart   # Restart all services
    19→  bash skills/docker/run.sh logs      # Tail logs from all services
    20→  bash skills/docker/run.sh ps        # Show running containers and health
    21→  bash skills/docker/run.sh build     # Rebuild images without starting
    22→  bash skills/docker/run.sh reset     # ⚠ Destroy all containers + volumes
    23→  bash skills/docker/run.sh migrate   # Run Alembic migrations inside the API container
    24→  bash skills/docker/run.sh shell     # Open a bash shell inside the API container
    25→
    26→Environment:
    27→  Copy .env.example → .env in the repo root to customise Postgres credentials.
    28→EOF
    29→}
    30→
    31→function ensure_env() {
    32→    if [[ ! -f ".env" ]]; then
    33→        echo "  No .env found — copying from .env.example"
    34→        cp .env.example .env
    35→    fi
    36→}
    37→
    38→case "$ACTION" in
    39→    up)
    40→        echo "=== docker: Starting all services ==="
    41→        ensure_env
    42→        docker compose up --build -d
    43→        echo ""
    44→        echo "Services:"
    45→        echo "  API      → http://localhost:8000"
    46→        echo "  API docs → http://localhost:8000/docs"
    47→        echo "  Postgres → localhost:5432"
    48→        ;;
    49→
    50→    down)
    51→        echo "=== docker: Stopping all services ==="
    52→        docker compose down
    53→        ;;
    54→
    55→    restart)
    56→        echo "=== docker: Restarting all services ==="
    57→        docker compose restart
    58→        ;;
    59→
    60→    logs)
    61→        SERVICE="${2:-}"
    62→        if [[ -n "$SERVICE" ]]; then
    63→            docker compose logs -f "$SERVICE"
    64→        else
    65→            docker compose logs -f
    66→        fi
    67→        ;;
    68→
    69→    ps)
    70→        echo "=== docker: Container status ==="
    71→        docker compose ps
    72→        ;;
    73→
    74→    build)
    75→        echo "=== docker: Building images ==="
    76→        docker compose build
    77→        ;;
    78→
    79→    reset)
    80→        echo "=== docker: ⚠ Resetting all containers and volumes ==="
    81→        read -r -p "This will DELETE all Postgres and ChromaDB data. Continue? [y/N] " confirm
    82→        if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    83→            docker compose down -v --remove-orphans
    84→            echo "Done. All volumes removed."
    85→        else
    86→            echo "Aborted."
    87→        fi
    88→        ;;
    89→
    90→    migrate)
    91→        echo "=== docker: Running Alembic migrations ==="
    92→        docker compose exec api alembic upgrade head
    93→        ;;
    94→
    95→    shell)
    96→        echo "=== docker: Opening shell in API container ==="
    97→        docker compose exec api bash
    98→        ;;
    99→
   100→    help|--help|-h)
   101→        show_help
   102→        ;;
   103→
   104→    *)
   105→        echo "Error: Unknown action '$ACTION'"
   106→        echo ""
   107→        show_help
   108→        exit 1
   109→        ;;
   110→esac
   111→

1→from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
     2→
     3→from fastapi import FastAPI
     4→
     5→from app.api.routes import api_router
     6→from app.core.config import settings
     7→from app.core.logging import get_logger, setup_logging
     8→
     9→setup_logging()
    10→logger = get_logger(__name__)
    11→
    12→
    13→@asynccontextmanager
    14→async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    15→    logger.info("starting up", app=settings.APP_NAME, version=settings.APP_VERSION)
    16→    yield
    17→    logger.info("shutting down")
    18→
    19→
    20→app = FastAPI(
    21→    title=settings.APP_NAME,
    22→    version=settings.APP_VERSION,
    23→    description="Backend API with AI memory integration",
    24→    lifespan=lifespan,
    25→)
    26→
    27→app.include_router(api_router)
    28→

1→# CLAUDE.md — Agent Operating Manual
     2→
     3→This repo is designed for **multi-agent collaboration**. Multiple AI agents
     4→may work on different parts of the codebase simultaneously. Follow these
     5→rules to avoid conflicts and maintain consistency.
     6→
     7→## Repo Map
     8→
     9→```
    10→/apps/web/          → Next.js frontend (TypeScript, Tailwind)
    11→/apps/api/          → FastAPI backend (Python, SQLAlchemy, Alembic)
    12→/packages/shared-types/ → Auto-generated TS types from OpenAPI
    13→/packages/ui/       → Shared React components
    14→/skills/            → Executable skill scripts for agents (read SKILLS_REGISTRY.md)
    15→/rules/             → AI rule files for structured feature development (PRD workflow)
    16→/tasks/             → Generated PRD.md and TASKS.md (gitignored per-feature work)
    17→```
    18→
    19→## Package Managers
    20→- **JavaScript/TypeScript**: `pnpm` (never npm or yarn)
    21→- **Python**: `uv` (never pip or poetry)
    22→
    23→## Skills System
    24→Before performing common operations, check `/skills/SKILLS_REGISTRY.md`.
    25→Each skill has a `manifest.json` with trigger conditions, commands, and risk levels.
    26→Execute skills via: `bash skills/<name>/run.sh [args]`
    27→
    28→Key skills:
    29→- **`finish` — run this when any task is complete. Lints, tests, commits, pushes. Mandatory.**
    30→- `type-sync` — after changing Pydantic schemas
    31→- `db-migrate` — after changing SQLAlchemy models
    32→- `openapi-gen` — after changing API routes or schemas
    33→- `dependency-add` — to add packages correctly
    34→- `lint-fix` — before committing
    35→- `test-run` — after completing work
    36→- `prd-workflow` — **start here for new features** (PRD → tasks → execution)
    37→- `deploy` — emergency manual deploy to Google Cloud Run (prefer CI/CD)
    38→
    39→## PRD Workflow (Structured Feature Development)
    40→
    41→Before writing any code for a new feature, use the 3-step PRD workflow:
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→bash skills/prd-workflow/run.sh init   # prints exact agent instructions
    45→```
    46→
    47→Then in your agent session, reference rule files in order:
    48→
    49→1. **Define** — `@rules/generate_prd.md` + describe feature → produces `tasks/PRD.md`
    50→2. **Plan**   — `@rules/generate_tasks.md @tasks/PRD.md`   → produces `tasks/TASKS.md`
    51→3. **Build**  — `@rules/task_list_management.md @tasks/TASKS.md` → executes one task at a time
    52→
    53→Rule files are in `/rules/`. Task files are written to `/tasks/` (not committed by default).
    54→See `skills/prd-workflow/README.md` for the full rationale.
    55→
    56→## Multi-Agent Coordination Rules
    57→
    58→### 1. Ownership Boundaries
    59→Agents MUST stay within their assigned scope. The codebase is divided into zones:
    60→
    61→| Zone | Path | Primary Language |
    62→|------|------|-----------------|
    63→| Frontend | `apps/web/` | TypeScript |
    64→| Backend | `apps/api/` | Python |
    65→| Shared Types | `packages/shared-types/` | TypeScript |
    66→| UI Library | `packages/ui/` | TypeScript |
    67→| Skills | `skills/` | Bash + JSON |
    68→
    69→### 2. File Locking Convention
    70→Before modifying a file, check if another agent is working on it.
    71→If you see a `// AGENT-LOCK: <agent-id>` comment at the top of a file,
    72→do not modify it until the lock is removed.
    73→
    74→### 3. Shared Resource Protocol
    75→These files affect the entire repo. Modifications require extra care:
    76→- `openapi.json` — regenerated by `type-sync` skill, do not edit manually
    77→- `package.json` (root) — only modify via `pnpm add`
    78→- `pyproject.toml` (root) — only modify for workspace config
    79→- `turbo.json` — only modify to add/change task definitions
    80→
    81→### 4. Type Contract
    82→The Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript pipeline is the contract between
    83→backend and frontend. After any schema change:
    84→1. Run `bash skills/type-sync/run.sh`
    85→2. Do NOT manually edit `packages/shared-types/src/api-types.ts`
    86→
    87→### 5. Commit Conventions
    88→- Use conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `docs:`, `chore:`
    89→- Scope to the affected zone: `feat(api): add user endpoint`
    90→- One logical change per commit
    91→
    92→## Backend Patterns
    93→- Config: `app.core.config.settings` (Pydantic Settings, reads `.env`)
    94→- Logging: `from app.core.logging import get_logger` (never `print()`)
    95→- DB sessions: `Depends(get_db)` in route handlers
    96→- All endpoints need Pydantic request/response schemas
    97→
    98→## Frontend Patterns
    99→- Server Components by default, `"use client"` only when needed
   100→- API types from `@repo/shared-types`, never local type definitions

1→# Agent Steering Document — AI-Native Monorepo
     2→
     3→## PRIME DIRECTIVE
     4→
     5→You operate **fully autonomously**. When given a task:
     6→
     7→1. Do the work.
     8→2. When done, call the `finish` skill — it lints, tests, commits, and pushes automatically.
     9→3. **Never ask the user to commit, push, or run any command.** Do it yourself.
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→bash skills/finish/run.sh "feat(api): describe what you did"
    13→```
    14→
    15→The finish skill is mandatory at the end of every task. No exceptions.
    16→If tests fail, fix them. If lint fails, it auto-fixes. Then push.
    17→
    18→## Repository Layout
    19→
    20→```
    21→/apps/web/              → Next.js frontend (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS)
    22→/apps/api/              → FastAPI backend (Python 3.11+, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy)
    23→/packages/shared-types/ → Auto-generated TypeScript API types & Zod schemas
    24→/packages/ui/           → Shared React component library
    25→/skills/                → Executable agent skills (read SKILLS_REGISTRY.md first)
    26→```
    27→
    28→## Rules for AI Agents
    29→
    30→### 1. Skills System (Read First)
    31→Before performing common operations, read `/skills/SKILLS_REGISTRY.md`.
    32→Each skill has a `manifest.json` with trigger conditions and a `run.sh` entry point.
    33→Execute skills via: `bash skills/<name>/run.sh [args]`
    34→
    35→**Mandatory triggers:**
    36→- Changed `apps/api/app/schemas/` → run `type-sync` skill
    37→- Changed `apps/api/app/models/` → run `db-migrate` skill
    38→- **Task complete** → run `finish` skill (lint + test + commit + push)
    39→- Need a package → run `dependency-add` skill (never run npm/pip directly)
    40→- Need the local stack running → run `docker up` skill
    41→
    42→### 2. Package Managers
    43→- Use **pnpm** for all Node/TypeScript dependencies.
    44→- Use **uv** for all Python dependencies.
    45→- Never use npm, yarn, pip, or poetry in this repo.
    46→
    47→### 3. Project Locations
    48→- The frontend lives in `/apps/web` and uses Next.js App Router.
    49→- The backend lives in `/apps/api` and uses FastAPI with Pydantic models.
    50→
    51→### 4. Type Synchronization (Critical)
    52→Any changes to FastAPI Pydantic models **require** running the `type-sync` skill:
    53→```bash
    54→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
    55→```
    56→
    57→### 5. Build Orchestration
    58→- Use `pnpm build` at the root to build all JS/TS packages via Turborepo.
    59→- Use `pnpm dev` at the root to start all dev servers.
    60→- Use `uv run` inside `/apps/api/` for Python commands.
    61→
    62→### 6. Environment Variables
    63→- Never commit `.env` files. Use `.env.example` as a template.
    64→- Backend config lives in `/apps/api/app/core/config.py` using Pydantic Settings.
    65→- Frontend env vars must be prefixed with `NEXT_PUBLIC_` if client-accessible.
    66→
    67→### 7. Database Migrations
    68→Run the `db-migrate` skill after modifying SQLAlchemy models:
    69→```bash
    70→bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "add users table"
    71→```
    72→
    73→### 8. Code Style
    74→- Python: follow existing patterns in `/apps/api/app/`. Use type hints everywhere.
    75→- TypeScript: strict mode is enabled. No `any` types.
    76→- All new API endpoints must have Pydantic request/response models.
    77→
    78→### 9. Logging
    79→- Use the structured logger from `app.core.logging` — never use `print()`.
    80→- Log levels: DEBUG for dev tracing, INFO for business events, WARNING for recoverable issues, ERROR for failures.
    81→- Supports kwargs: `logger.info("event", user_id=42, action="login")`
    82→
    83→### 10. AI Memory (ChromaDB — Local Vector DB)
    84→- Local semantic memory via ChromaDB. Zero external dependencies.
    85→- Service: `/apps/api/app/services/memory.py` → `MemoryService` class.
    86→- API endpoints: `POST /memory/add`, `POST /memory/search`.
    87→- **Skill**: `bash skills/memory/run.sh save|recall|list` — use this to persist agent decisions and recall context.
    88→- Data is stored in `.data/chromadb/` (gitignored). Persists across sessions.
    89→- Use tags to namespace memories: `agent_backend`, `user_42`, `decisions`, etc.
    90→
    91→### 11. Context Versioning (Entire CLI)
    92→- Entire captures agent reasoning at every git commit via hooks.
    93→- Run `bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "description"` at logical milestones.
    94→- Browse past context: `entire log`
    95→
    96→### 12. Multi-Agent Coordination
    97→- Stay within your assigned zone (see CLAUDE.md for boundaries).
    98→- Do not manually edit auto-generated files (`openapi.json`, `api-types.ts`).
    99→- Use conventional commits scoped to zones: `feat(api): add user endpoint`.
   100→

name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

# Never cancel an in-flight deploy — always let it finish.
concurrency:
  group: deploy-production
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  GCP_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}
  GCP_REGION:     ${{ vars.GCP_REGION || 'us-central1' }}
  GAR_LOCATION:   ${{ vars.GAR_LOCATION || 'us-central1' }}
  GAR_REPO:       ${{ vars.GAR_REPO || 'monorepo' }}

jobs:
  # ── Deploy API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  deploy-api:
    name: Deploy API → Cloud Run
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write   # Required for Workload Identity Federation

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Authenticate to GCP (Workload Identity Federation)
        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
        with:
          workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.WIF_PROVIDER }}
          service_account: ${{ secrets.WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

      - name: Set up gcloud CLI
        uses: google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2

      - name: Configure Docker auth for Artifact Registry
        run: gcloud auth configure-docker ${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev --quiet

      - name: Set image tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
          SHORT="${SHA:0:7}"
          BASE="${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev/${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}/${{ env.GAR_REPO }}/api"
          echo "image=${BASE}:${SHORT}"       >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "latest=${BASE}:latest"         >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build context: repo root (required for pnpm workspace packages)

# ── Stage 1: Install dependencies ─────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.30.0 --activate
WORKDIR /app

# Copy workspace manifests first for better layer caching.
# pnpm needs the full workspace graph to resolve internal packages.
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml ./
COPY apps/web/package.json                      ./apps/web/
COPY packages/shared-types/package.json         ./packages/shared-types/
COPY packages/ui/package.json                   ./packages/ui/

RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# ── Stage 2: Build ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.30.0 --activate

# ─── Builder stage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder

# Install uv for fast dependency resolution
COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /usr/local/bin/uv

WORKDIR /app

# Build context is repo root so uv.lock is available
COPY uv.lock pyproject.toml ./
COPY apps/api/ ./apps/api/

# Install all API deps — venv is created at /app/.venv (workspace root)
RUN uv sync --no-dev --all-packages

# ─── Runtime stage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.11-slim AS runtime

# curl for health check; psycopg2-binary bundles its own libpq so no libpq-dev needed
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \

content.text:
Design an implementation plan for making this monorepo into a reusable template that users can customize for their own projects.

## Context
This is a monorepo at /Users/ujjwalraj/Developer/Experiments/AgentOptimisedMonorepo with:
- Next.js frontend (apps/web/)
- FastAPI backend (apps/api/)
- Shared packages (@repo/shared-types, @repo/ui)
- Skills system (skills/)
- Docker, CI/CD, GCP deployment

The user wants anyone to be able to clone this repo and customize it for their own application — naming it whatever they want, for any use case.

## Hardcoded values that need templatizing

| Item | Location | Current Value |
|------|----------|---------------|
| Project Name | package.json, pyproject.toml, Makefile comment, AGENTS.md | "ai-native-monorepo" / "AI-Native Monorepo" |
| Package Scope | @repo/* in shared-types, ui, web deps | "@repo" |
| API App Name | apps/api/app/core/config.py | "AI-Native Monorepo API" |
| API Description | apps/api/app/main.py | "Backend API with AI memory integration" |
| Web Title | apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx | "Create Next App" |
| Web Description | apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx | "Generated by create next app" |
| DB defaults | docker-compose.yml, .env.example | "monorepo" (user/pass/db) |
| Container names | docker-compose.yml | "monorepo_postgres", "monorepo_api" |
| GAR Repo | Makefile, deploy.yml, deploy/run.sh | "monorepo" |
| GitHub URL | README.md | "github.com/whyujjwal/AgentOptimisedMonorepo" |
| .env.example | apps/api/.env.example | APP_NAME=AI-Native Monorepo API |

## Requirements
1. A user clones the repo, runs ONE command, answers a few prompts, and everything is renamed/configured
2. Should be a new `scaffold` or `init` skill (bash script in skills/init/)
3. The script should:
   - Prompt for: project name (human-readable), slug (kebab-case), package scope (@org), description
   - Auto-derive what it can (slug from name, container names from slug, etc.)
   - Use sed to replace all hardcoded values across the repo
   - Update .env.example files
   - Optionally run `make setup` after
   - Print a summary of changes
4. Should also create a GitHub template repo mechanism (template.json or similar) if useful
5. The existing skills system (manifest.json pattern) should be followed

## Design constraints
- Keep it simple — a bash script with sed replacements, no external template engines
- Must be idempotent-safe (don't break if run twice)
- Should handle the @repo scope rename carefully (affects imports in TS files too)
- Must update pnpm-workspace.yaml if the scope changes affect it
- Should NOT touch lock files (those get regenerated)

Please design a detailed implementation plan including the exact files to create/modify, the script logic, and the sed replacement patterns.

1→# Skills Registry
     2→
     3→This file is the **central index** of all available skills in this monorepo.
     4→AI agents should read this file to discover what skills exist, what they do,
     5→and when to use them. Each skill is a self-contained directory under `/skills/`.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## How Skills Work
    10→
    11→Each skill directory contains:
    12→- `manifest.json` — Machine-readable metadata (trigger conditions, commands, inputs/outputs)
    13→- `README.md` — Human/agent-readable explanation of when and how to use the skill
    14→- `run.sh` — Executable entry point (always run from repo root)
    15→
    16→Agents should:
    17→1. Read this registry to find relevant skills for their current task.
    18→2. Read the skill's `manifest.json` to understand inputs, outputs, and commands.
    19→3. Execute via `bash skills/<skill-name>/run.sh [args]` from the repo root.
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Available Skills
    24→
    25→| Skill | Trigger | Description |
    26→|-------|---------|-------------|
    27→| `finish` | **Every task completion** | Lint → test → commit → push. The mandatory last step. Never skip. |
    28→| `type-sync` | Pydantic model changed | Regenerates OpenAPI spec → TypeScript types → validates frontend build |
    29→| `db-migrate` | SQLAlchemy model changed | Creates and applies Alembic migration |
    30→| `openapi-gen` | Any schema change | Exports OpenAPI JSON from FastAPI app |
    31→| `lint-fix` | Before commit / on demand | Runs linters and auto-fixes across the monorepo |
    32→| `test-run` | After code changes | Runs test suites for affected packages |
    33→| `dependency-add` | Need to add a package | Adds dependency using correct package manager (pnpm or uv) |
    34→| `memory` | Any time | Save important context or recall past decisions via local vector DB |
    35→| `docker` | On demand | Manage full stack (API + PostgreSQL) via Docker Compose |
    36→| `checkpoint` | At logical milestones | Creates a versioned Entire snapshot of codebase + agent context |
    37→| `prd-workflow` | Starting a new feature | Ryan Carson's 3-step workflow: PRD → task list → one-task-at-a-time execution |
    38→| `deploy` | Emergency manual deploy | Build Docker image and deploy API or Web to Google Cloud Run via gcloud |
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## Skill Trigger Rules
    43→
    44→Agents MUST check these trigger conditions after making changes:
    45→
    46→0. **Task complete** → Run `finish "<conventional commit message>"` — always, no exceptions
    47→1. **Modified a file in `apps/api/app/schemas/`** → Run `type-sync`
    48→2. **Modified a file in `apps/api/app/models/`** → Run `db-migrate`
    49→3. **Modified any Python or TypeScript file** → Run `lint-fix`
    50→4. **Finished a feature or fix** → Run `test-run`
    51→5. **Need a new library** → Run `dependency-add` (never run npm/pip directly)
    52→6. **Completed a logical milestone** → Run `checkpoint` to version agent context
    53→7. **Want to remember a decision, pattern, or preference** → Run `memory save`
    54→8. **Need past context before starting a task** → Run `memory recall`
    55→9. **Need to start or manage the local stack** → Run `docker up|down|migrate`
    56→10. **Starting work on a new feature** → Run `prd-workflow init` and follow the 3-step rule-file workflow
    57→11. **Need to deploy manually outside CI/CD** → Run `deploy api|web` (requires gcloud auth; prefer pushing to `main`)
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## PRD Workflow (Rule Files)
    62→
    63→The `prd-workflow` skill orchestrates three AI rule files that enforce structured feature development:
    64→
    65→| Rule File | When to Use | Output |
    66→|-----------|-------------|--------|
    67→| `rules/generate_prd.md` | Beginning of a feature | `tasks/PRD.md` |
    68→| `rules/generate_tasks.md` | After PRD is written | `tasks/TASKS.md` |
    69→| `rules/task_list_management.md` | During implementation | Updated `tasks/TASKS.md` |
    70→
    71→**In your agent**, reference rule files like this:
    72→```
    73→@rules/generate_prd.md I want to build: <describe feature>
    74→@rules/generate_tasks.md @tasks/PRD.md
    75→@rules/task_list_management.md @tasks/TASKS.md
    76→```
    77→
    78→---
    79→
    80→## AI Infrastructure
    81→
    82→### Long-Term Memory (ChromaDB)
    83→Agents can store and retrieve semantic memories via the `memory` skill, the `/memory/add` and
    84→`/memory/search` API endpoints, or directly through `app.services.memory.MemoryService`.
    85→ChromaDB runs locally — no external API, no account required. Data persists in `.data/chromadb/`.
    86→Use tags to namespace memories per agent or user.
    87→
    88→```bash
    89→# Save
    90→bash skills/memory/run.sh save "content" "tag1,tag2"
    91→# Recall
    92→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "query" "tag"
    93→# List
    94→bash skills/memory/run.sh list "tag"
    95→```
    96→
    97→### Context Versioning (Entire CLI)
    98→Entire captures agent reasoning at every git commit via hooks in `.claude/settings.json`.
    99→- Strategy: `manual-commit` — checkpoints happen on git commits
   100→- Browse history: `entire log`
   101→- Compare checkpoints: `entire diff <id>`
   102→- Use the `checkpoint` skill at logical milestones
   103→
