1→# ── AI-Native Monorepo — Local Dev Entrypoint ─────────────────────────────────
     2→# Run `make help` to see all available targets.
     3→#
     4→# Package managers: pnpm (JS/TS), uv (Python) — never npm/pip directly.
     5→# See CLAUDE.md for conventions and skills/SKILLS_REGISTRY.md for skill docs.
     6→
     7→# GCP config — override per invocation: make deploy-api GCP_PROJECT=my-project
     8→GCP_PROJECT  ?= $(shell gcloud config get-value project 2>/dev/null || echo "")
     9→GCP_REGION   ?= us-central1
    10→GAR_LOCATION ?= us-central1
    11→GAR_REPO     ?= monorepo
    12→IMAGE_TAG    ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
    13→
    14→UV      := /opt/homebrew/bin/uv
    15→PNPM    := pnpm
    16→TURBO   := $(PNPM) turbo
    17→
    18→.DEFAULT_GOAL := help
    19→.PHONY: help setup dev build test lint \
    20→        docker-up docker-down docker-logs docker-ps docker-reset \
    21→        migrate migrate-gen type-sync \
    22→        finish \
    23→        deploy-api deploy-web \
    24→        clean
    25→
    26→# ── Help ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→help: ## Show this help message
    28→	@awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\033[1mUsage:\033[0m make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n\n\033[1mTargets:\033[0m\n"} \
    29→	/^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-20s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 }' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)
    30→
    31→# ── Setup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→setup: ## First-time setup: copy .env files and install all dependencies
    33→	@echo "==> Copying .env files (skipping existing)..."
    34→	@[ -f .env ]              || cp .env.example .env
    35→	@[ -f apps/api/.env ]     || cp apps/api/.env.example apps/api/.env
    36→	@[ -f apps/web/.env.local ] || cp apps/web/.env.example apps/web/.env.local
    37→	@echo "==> Installing JS dependencies..."
    38→	$(PNPM) install
    39→	@echo "==> Installing Python dependencies (including dev)..."
    40→	cd apps/api && $(UV) sync --dev
    41→	@echo ""
    42→	@echo "✓ Setup complete. Edit .env files with your config before running \`make dev\`."
    43→
    44→# ── Development ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→dev: ## Start full local stack: Postgres+API via Docker, web via Next.js dev server
    46→	@echo "==> Starting Docker stack (API + Postgres)..."
    47→	bash skills/docker/run.sh up
    48→	@echo "==> Starting web dev server..."
    49→	$(TURBO) run dev --filter=web
    50→
    51→build: ## Build all packages via Turborepo
    52→	$(TURBO) run build
    53→
    54→# ── Testing & Linting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55→test: ## Run all tests (Python pytest + JS build check)
    56→	bash skills/test-run/run.sh
    57→
    58→test-api: ## Run Python tests only
    59→	cd apps/api && $(UV) run pytest -v
    60→
    61→test-web: ## Run Next.js build check (smoke test until JS tests are added)
    62→	NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000 $(TURBO) run build --filter=web
    63→
    64→lint: ## Lint and auto-fix all code (Python ruff + JS eslint)
    65→	bash skills/lint-fix/run.sh
    66→
    67→lint-check: ## Lint check only, no auto-fix (used in CI)
    68→	cd apps/api && $(UV) run ruff check app/
    69→	cd apps/api && $(UV) run ruff format --check app/
    70→	$(TURBO) run lint
    71→
    72→type-check: ## Run TypeScript type-checking across all packages
    73→	$(TURBO) run type-check
    74→
    75→# ── Docker ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    76→docker-up: ## Start Docker stack (API + Postgres)
    77→	bash skills/docker/run.sh up
    78→
    79→docker-down: ## Stop Docker stack
    80→	bash skills/docker/run.sh down
    81→
    82→docker-logs: ## Tail Docker logs (SERVICE=api|postgres to filter)
    83→	bash skills/docker/run.sh logs $(SERVICE)
    84→
    85→docker-ps: ## Show container status
    86→	bash skills/docker/run.sh ps
    87→
    88→docker-reset: ## Destroy all containers AND volumes — data loss!
    89→	bash skills/docker/run.sh reset
    90→
    91→# ── Database ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92→migrate: ## Apply all pending Alembic migrations (local)
    93→	cd apps/api && $(UV) run alembic upgrade head
    94→
    95→migrate-gen: ## Generate a new migration (MSG="description" required)
    96→	@[ -n "$(MSG)" ] || (echo "Error: MSG is required. Usage: make migrate-gen MSG=\"add users table\"" && exit 1)
    97→	bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "$(MSG)"
    98→
    99→migrate-down: ## Roll back the last Alembic migration (local)
   100→	cd apps/api && $(UV) run alembic downgrade -1
   101→
   102→# ── Type Sync ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103→type-sync: ## Regenerate TypeScript types from Pydantic schemas
   104→	bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   105→
   106→# ── Finish (autonomous task completion) ────────────────────────────────────────
   107→finish: ## Lint → test → commit → push. MSG="feat(api): ..." required.
   108→	@[ -n "$(MSG)" ] || (echo "Error: MSG is required. Usage: make finish MSG=\"feat(api): add endpoint\"" && exit 1)
   109→	bash skills/finish/run.sh "$(MSG)"
   110→
   111→# ── Deployment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→deploy-api: ## Deploy API to Cloud Run (requires gcloud auth)
   113→	GCP_PROJECT=$(GCP_PROJECT) GCP_REGION=$(GCP_REGION) \
   114→	GAR_LOCATION=$(GAR_LOCATION) GAR_REPO=$(GAR_REPO) IMAGE_TAG=$(IMAGE_TAG) \
   115→	bash skills/deploy/run.sh api
   116→
   117→deploy-web: ## Deploy Web to Cloud Run (requires gcloud auth, API must be deployed)
   118→	GCP_PROJECT=$(GCP_PROJECT) GCP_REGION=$(GCP_REGION) \
   119→	GAR_LOCATION=$(GAR_LOCATION) GAR_REPO=$(GAR_REPO) IMAGE_TAG=$(IMAGE_TAG) \
   120→	bash skills/deploy/run.sh web
   121→
   122→deploy: ## Deploy both API and Web sequentially to Cloud Run
   123→	GCP_PROJECT=$(GCP_PROJECT) GCP_REGION=$(GCP_REGION) \
   124→	GAR_LOCATION=$(GAR_LOCATION) GAR_REPO=$(GAR_REPO) IMAGE_TAG=$(IMAGE_TAG) \
   125→	bash skills/deploy/run.sh all
   126→
   127→# ── Cleanup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   128→clean: ## Remove build artifacts and caches
   129→	$(TURBO) run clean --if-present || true
   130→	find . -type d -name __pycache__ -not -path './.venv/*' -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
   131→	find . -name "*.pyc" -not -path './.venv/*' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
   132→	rm -rf apps/web/.next apps/web/out
   133→	rm -rf packages/shared-types/dist packages/ui/dist
   134→	@echo "✓ Clean complete."
   135→

1→# Agent Steering Document — AI-Native Monorepo
     2→
     3→## PRIME DIRECTIVE
     4→
     5→You operate **fully autonomously**. When given a task:
     6→
     7→1. Do the work.
     8→2. When done, call the `finish` skill — it lints, tests, commits, and pushes automatically.
     9→3. **Never ask the user to commit, push, or run any command.** Do it yourself.
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→bash skills/finish/run.sh "feat(api): describe what you did"
    13→```
    14→
    15→The finish skill is mandatory at the end of every task. No exceptions.
    16→If tests fail, fix them. If lint fails, it auto-fixes. Then push.
    17→
    18→## Repository Layout
    19→
    20→```
    21→/apps/web/              → Next.js frontend (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS)
    22→/apps/api/              → FastAPI backend (Python 3.11+, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy)
    23→/packages/shared-types/ → Auto-generated TypeScript API types & Zod schemas
    24→/packages/ui/           → Shared React component library
    25→/skills/                → Executable agent skills (read SKILLS_REGISTRY.md first)
    26→```
    27→

     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→
     7→# Never cancel an in-flight deploy — always let it finish.
     8→concurrency:
     9→  group: deploy-production
    10→  cancel-in-progress: false
    11→
    12→env:
    13→  GCP_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}
    14→  GCP_REGION:     ${{ vars.GCP_REGION || 'us-central1' }}
    15→  GAR_LOCATION:   ${{ vars.GAR_LOCATION || 'us-central1' }}
    16→  GAR_REPO:       ${{ vars.GAR_REPO || 'monorepo' }}

    18→jobs:
    19→  # ── Deploy API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→  deploy-api:
    21→    name: Deploy API → Cloud Run
    22→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    23→    environment: production
    24→    permissions:
    25→      contents: read
    26→      id-token: write   # Required for Workload Identity Federation
    27→
    28→    steps:
    29→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    30→
    31→      - name: Authenticate to GCP (Workload Identity Federation)
    32→        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
    33→        with:
    34→          workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.WIF_PROVIDER }}
    35→          service_account: ${{ secrets.WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}
    36→
    37→      - name: Set up gcloud CLI

    39→
    40→      - name: Configure Docker auth for Artifact Registry
    41→        run: gcloud auth configure-docker ${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev --quiet
    42→
    43→      - name: Set image tag
    44→        id: tag
    45→        run: |
    46→          SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
    47→          SHORT="${SHA:0:7}"
    48→          BASE="${{ env.GAR_LOCATION }}-docker.pkg.dev/${{ env.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}/${{ env.GAR_REPO }}/api"
    49→          echo "image=${BASE}:${SHORT}"       >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    50→          echo "latest=${BASE}:latest"         >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

    39→=== Deploy Skill ===
    40→
    41→Deploy API or Web service to Google Cloud Run.
    42→
    43→Usage:
    44→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh api                  # Deploy API
    45→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh web                  # Deploy Web (auto-detects API URL)
    46→  bash skills/deploy/run.sh all                  # Deploy API then Web
    47→
    48→Environment overrides:
    49→  GCP_PROJECT     GCP project ID (default: gcloud config)
    50→  GCP_REGION      Cloud Run region (default: us-central1)
    51→  GAR_LOCATION    Artifact Registry location (default: us-central1)
    52→  GAR_REPO        Artifact Registry repo name (default: monorepo)
    53→  IMAGE_TAG       Docker image tag (default: git SHA short)
    54→  PROD_API_URL    API URL baked into web image (default: auto-detect from Cloud Run)
    55→
    56→Prerequisites:

    59→  gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID
    60→
    61→WARNING: For production, prefer pushing to main to trigger the GitHub Actions
    62→         deploy workflow. Use this skill for emergency deploys only.
    63→EOF
    64→}

   115→        echo "=== deploy/web: Auto-detecting API URL from Cloud Run ==="
   116→        prod_api_url="$(gcloud run services describe "$API_SERVICE" \
   117→            --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   118→            --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   119→            --format "value(status.url)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
   120→        if [[ -z "$prod_api_url" ]]; then
   121→            echo "ERROR: Could not detect API URL. Deploy the API first, or set PROD_API_URL."
   122→            exit 1
   123→        fi
   124→        echo "  Detected: $prod_api_url"
   125→    fi
   126→
   127→    local web_image="${GAR_BASE}/${WEB_SERVICE}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
   128→    local web_latest="${GAR_BASE}/${WEB_SERVICE}:latest"
   129→
   130→    echo ""
   131→    echo "=== deploy/web: Building image ==="
   132→    echo "  Image: $web_image"
   133→    echo "  NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: $prod_api_url"
   134→    docker build \
   135→        -f apps/web/Dockerfile \
   136→        --build-arg "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=${prod_api_url}" \
   137→        -t "$web_image" \
   138→        -t "$web_latest" \
   139→        .
   140→
   141→    echo ""
   142→    echo "=== deploy/web: Pushing image ==="
   143→    docker push "$web_image"
   144→    docker push "$web_latest"
   145→
   146→    echo ""
   147→    echo "=== deploy/web: Deploying to Cloud Run ==="
   148→    gcloud run deploy "$WEB_SERVICE" \
   149→        --image "$web_image" \
   150→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   151→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   152→        --platform managed \
   153→        --allow-unauthenticated \
   154→        --min-instances 0 \
   155→        --max-instances 5 \
   156→        --memory 512Mi \
   157→        --cpu 1 \
   158→        --timeout 60 \
   159→        --concurrency 80
   160→
   161→    echo ""
   162→    echo "=== deploy/web: Done ==="
   163→    local url
   164→    url="$(gcloud run services describe "$WEB_SERVICE" \
   165→        --region "$GCP_REGION" \
   166→        --project "$GCP_PROJECT" \
   167→        --format "value(status.url)")"
   168→    echo "  URL: $url"
   169→}
   170→
   171→case "$TARGET" in
   172→    api)
   173→        check_prereqs
   174→        deploy_api
   175→        ;;
   176→    web)
   177→        check_prereqs
   178→        deploy_web
   179→        ;;
   180→    all)
   181→        check_prereqs
   182→        deploy_api
   183→        deploy_web
   184→        ;;
   185→    help|--help|-h)
   186→        show_help
   187→        ;;
   188→    *)
   189→        echo "Error: Unknown target '$TARGET'"
   190→        echo ""
   191→        show_help
   192→        exit 1
   193→        ;;
   194→esac
   195→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# finish — autonomous task completion
     3→# Usage: bash skills/finish/run.sh "feat(api): add user auth"
     4→# Runs: lint → test → commit → push
     5→set -euo pipefail
     6→
     7→REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
     8→cd "$REPO_ROOT"
     9→
    10→MESSAGE="${1:?Usage: bash skills/finish/run.sh \"<conventional commit message>\"}"
    11→SKIP_TESTS="${2:-false}"
    12→
    13→UV=/opt/homebrew/bin/uv
    14→
    15→# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→green()  { echo -e "\033[0;32m$*\033[0m"; }
    17→yellow() { echo -e "\033[0;33m$*\033[0m"; }
    18→red()    { echo -e "\033[0;31m$*\033[0m"; }
    19→step()   { echo ""; echo "── $* ──────────────────────────────────────"; }
    20→
    21→# ── 1. Detect what changed ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22→step "1/5  Detecting changes"
    23→
    24→HAS_PYTHON=false
    25→HAS_JS=false
    26→
    27→if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^apps/api/' ||
    28→   git diff --cached --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^apps/api/' ||
    29→   git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^apps/api/'; then
    30→  HAS_PYTHON=true
    31→fi
    32→
    33→if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '^(apps/web|packages)/' ||
    34→   git diff --cached --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '^(apps/web|packages)/' ||
    35→   git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '^(apps/web|packages)/'; then
    36→  HAS_JS=true
    37→fi
    38→
    39→echo "  Python changes: $HAS_PYTHON"
    40→echo "  JS/TS changes:  $HAS_JS"
    41→
    42→# ── 2. Lint & auto-fix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→step "2/5  Lint"
    44→
    45→LINT_FAILED=false
    46→
    47→if [[ "$HAS_PYTHON" == "true" ]]; then
    48→  echo "  → Python: ruff fix + format"
    49→  cd apps/api
    50→  $UV run ruff check --fix app/ 2>&1 || LINT_FAILED=true
    51→  $UV run ruff format app/     2>&1 || LINT_FAILED=true
    52→  cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    53→fi
    54→
    55→if [[ "$HAS_JS" == "true" ]]; then
    56→  echo "  → JS/TS: eslint"
    57→  pnpm turbo run lint 2>&1 || LINT_FAILED=true
    58→fi
    59→
    60→if [[ "$LINT_FAILED" == "true" ]]; then
    61→  yellow "  Lint had issues but auto-fix was applied. Continuing."
    62→fi
    63→
    64→green "  ✓ Lint complete"
    65→
    66→# ── 3. Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67→step "3/5  Tests"
    68→
    69→if [[ "$SKIP_TESTS" == "true" ]]; then
    70→  yellow "  ⚠ Tests skipped (SKIP_TESTS=true)"
    71→else
    72→  TEST_FAILED=false
    73→
    74→  if [[ "$HAS_PYTHON" == "true" ]]; then
    75→    echo "  → Python: pytest"
    76→    cd apps/api
    77→    if $UV run pytest -q 2>&1; then
    78→      green "  ✓ Python tests passed"
    79→    else
    80→      EXIT_CODE=$?
    81→      # pytest exit code 5 = no tests collected — that's fine
    82→      if [[ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 5 ]]; then
    83→        yellow "  ⚠ No Python tests found yet — skipping"
    84→      else
    85→        red "  ✗ Python tests FAILED"
    86→        TEST_FAILED=true
    87→      fi
    88→    fi
    89→    cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    90→  fi
    91→
    92→  if [[ "$TEST_FAILED" == "true" ]]; then
    93→    red ""
    94→    red "Tests failed. Fix them before pushing."
    95→    red "To skip tests (use only if no tests exist yet): bash skills/finish/run.sh \"$MESSAGE\" true"
    96→    exit 1
    97→  fi
    98→fi
    99→
   100→green "  ✓ Tests complete"
   101→
   102→# ── 4. Commit ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103→step "4/5  Commit"
   104→
   105→# Stage everything including lint auto-fixes
   106→git add -A
   107→
   108→if git diff --cached --quiet; then
   109→  yellow "  Nothing to commit — working tree clean"
   110→  COMMITTED=false
   111→else
   112→  git commit -m "$MESSAGE"
   113→  COMMITTED=true
   114→  green "  ✓ Committed: $(git rev-parse --short HEAD) — $MESSAGE"
   115→fi
   116→
   117→# ── 5. Push ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   118→step "5/5  Push"
   119→
   120→BRANCH="$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)"
   121→REMOTE="origin"
   122→
   123→# Ensure remote is reachable
   124→if ! git ls-remote --exit-code "$REMOTE" &>/dev/null; then
   125→  red "  Cannot reach remote '$REMOTE'. Check your network or git credentials."
   126→  exit 1
   127→fi
   128→
   129→git push "$REMOTE" "$BRANCH"
   130→
   131→green ""
   132→green "  ✓ Pushed to $REMOTE/$BRANCH"
   133→echo ""
   134→echo "  Branch:  $BRANCH"
   135→echo "  Remote:  $(git remote get-url $REMOTE)"
   136→if [[ "$COMMITTED" == "true" ]]; then
   137→  echo "  Commit:  $(git rev-parse --short HEAD) — $MESSAGE"
   138→fi
   139→echo ""
   140→green "=== finish: Done. CI will run automatically on push. ==="
   141→

content.content:
1→# AI-Native Monorepo
     2→
     3→A production-ready monorepo architected from the ground up for **human and AI agent collaboration**. It is not just a codebase — it is an environment where AI agents can autonomously read, reason, write code, remember context, and coordinate across services without stepping on each other.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Use as a Template
     8→
     9→Clone this repo and run the init script to make it your own:
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→git clone https://github.com/whyujjwal/AgentOptimisedMonorepo.git my-project
    13→cd my-project
    14→bash skills/init/run.sh
    15→```
    16→
    17→The script will prompt for your project name, package scope, and description, then rename everything across the repo. You can also run it non-interactively:
    18→
    19→```bash
    20→PROJECT_NAME="My App" PROJECT_SLUG="my-app" PACKAGE_SCOPE="myorg" DESCRIPTION="My SaaS platform" \
    21→  bash skills/init/run.sh
    22→```
    23→
    24→See `skills/init/README.md` for details on what gets replaced.
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Table of Contents
    29→
    30→- [Why This Exists](#why-this-exists)
    31→- [Architecture at a Glance](#architecture-at-a-glance)
    32→- [Repository Structure](#repository-structure)
    33→- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
    34→  - [Option A — Docker (recommended)](#option-a--docker-recommended)
    35→  - [Option B — Local dev without Docker](#option-b--local-dev-without-docker)
    36→- [The Stack](#the-stack)
    37→  - [Backend — FastAPI](#backend--fastapi)
    38→  - [Frontend — Next.js](#frontend--nextjs)
    39→  - [Shared Types Pipeline](#shared-types-pipeline)
    40→  - [UI Library](#ui-library)
    41→- [The Skills System](#the-skills-system)
    42→  - [What Is a Skill?](#what-is-a-skill)
    43→  - [Available Skills](#available-skills)
    44→  - [When Agents Must Use Skills](#when-agents-must-use-skills)
    45→- [Agent Memory — Local Vector Database](#agent-memory--local-vector-database)
    46→  - [Why Agents Need Memory](#why-agents-need-memory)
    47→  - [How It Works Under the Hood](#how-it-works-under-the-hood)
    48→  - [Using Memory As a Developer](#using-memory-as-a-developer)
    49→  - [Using Memory As an Agent](#using-memory-as-an-agent)
    50→  - [Memory API Endpoints](#memory-api-endpoints)
    51→- [Multi-Agent Coordination](#multi-agent-coordination)
    52→  - [Zone Ownership](#zone-ownership)
    53→  - [The Type Contract](#the-type-contract)
    54→  - [Commit Conventions](#commit-conventions)
    55→- [Context Versioning with Entire](#context-versioning-with-entire)
    56→- [Docker](#docker)
    57→  - [Services](#services)
    58→  - [Docker Commands](#docker-commands)
    59→  - [Connecting to Postgres](#connecting-to-postgres)
    60→- [Backend Deep Dive](#backend-deep-dive)
    61→  - [Configuration](#configuration)
    62→  - [Structured Logging](#structured-logging)
    63→  - [Database Layer](#database-layer)
    64→  - [Adding an Endpoint](#adding-an-endpoint)
    65→- [Frontend Deep Dive](#frontend-deep-dive)
    66→- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
    67→- [Environment Variables Reference](#environment-variables-reference)
    68→
    69→---
    70→
    71→## Why This Exists
    72→
    73→Most monorepos are built for humans. This one is built for **both**.
    74→
    75→When you give an AI agent access to a codebase, several things break immediately:
    76→- The agent doesn't know *where* to put things
    77→- The agent runs npm when it should run pnpm, pip when it should run uv
    78→- After changing a backend schema, the frontend types silently go stale
    79→- Two agents working in parallel clobber each other's changes
    80→- The agent has no memory — every session starts from zero
    81→
    82→This monorepo solves all of those problems explicitly:
    83→
    84→| Problem | Solution |
    85→|---------|---------|
    86→| Where do things go? | `AGENTS.md` files in every zone, `CLAUDE.md` at the root |
    87→| Wrong package manager | `dependency-add` skill enforces pnpm/uv |
    88→| Stale types | `type-sync` skill is a mandatory trigger |
    89→| Parallel agent conflicts | Zone ownership boundaries + file lock conventions |
    90→| No memory between sessions | ChromaDB vector store via `memory` skill |
    91→| Lost reasoning context | `checkpoint` skill + Entire CLI hooks |
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→## Architecture at a Glance
    96→
    97→```
    98→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    99→│                        AI-Native Monorepo                            │
   100→│                                                                      │
   101→│  ┌─────────────────┐           ┌──────────────────────────────────┐  │
   102→│  │   apps/web      │           │          apps/api                │  │
   103→│  │                 │  HTTP/JSON│                                  │  │
   104→│  │  Next.js 16     │◄─────────►│  FastAPI + Python 3.11           │  │
   105→│  │  React 19       │           │  SQLAlchemy + Alembic            │  │
   106→│  │  Tailwind 4     │           │  ChromaDB (vector memory)        │  │
   107→│  │  App Router     │           │  Pydantic v2                     │  │
   108→│  └────────┬────────┘           └──────────────┬───────────────────┘  │
   109→│           │                                   │                      │
   110→│           │ imports                           │ exports              │
   111→│           ▼                                   ▼                      │
   112→│  ┌────────────────────┐      ┌───────────────────────────────────┐   │
   113→│  │ packages/ui        │      │  packages/shared-types            │   │
   114→│  │                    │      │                                   │   │
   115→│  │ React components   │      │  openapi.json (source of truth)   │   │
   116→│  │ Shared across apps │      │  → TypeScript interfaces          │   │
   117→│  └────────────────────┘      │  → Zod validation schemas         │   │
   118→│                              └───────────────────────────────────┘   │
   119→│                                                                      │
   120→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
   121→│  │  skills/  — Executable scripts for agents & humans            │   │
   122→│  │                                                               │   │
   123→│  │  type-sync  db-migrate  memory  checkpoint  dependency-add    │   │
   124→│  │  openapi-gen  lint-fix  test-run                              │   │
   125→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
   126→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   127→```
   128→
   129→---
   130→
   131→## Repository Structure
   132→
   133→```
   134→/
   135→├── AGENTS.md                  # AI agent instructions (root level)
   136→├── CLAUDE.md                  # Multi-agent coordination rules
   137→├── README.md                  # This file
   138→├── package.json               # Root workspace config
   139→├── pnpm-workspace.yaml        # pnpm workspaces
   140→├── turbo.json                 # Turborepo task pipeline
   141→├── pyproject.toml             # Python workspace config
   142→├── openapi.json               # Generated — do not edit manually
   143→│
   144→├── apps/
   145→│   ├── api/                   # FastAPI backend
   146→│   │   ├── AGENTS.md          # Backend agent instructions
   147→│   │   ├── pyproject.toml     # Python dependencies (uv)
   148→│   │   ├── alembic.ini        # Database migration config
   149→│   │   ├── alembic/
   150→│   │   │   └── versions/      # Migration files
   151→│   │   └── app/
   152→│   │       ├── main.py        # FastAPI app entry point
   153→│   │       ├── api/routes/    # HTTP route handlers
   154→│   │       ├── core/
   155→│   │       │   ├── config.py  # Settings via Pydantic
   156→│   │       │   ├── database.py# SQLAlchemy engine + session
   157→│   │       │   └── logging.py # Structured logger
   158→│   │       ├── models/        # SQLAlchemy ORM models
   159→│   │       ├── schemas/       # Pydantic request/response schemas
   160→│   │       ├── services/      # Business logic (incl. memory)
   161→│   │       └── scripts/       # CLI utilities
   162→│   │
   163→│   └── web/                   # Next.js frontend
   164→│       ├── AGENTS.md          # Frontend agent instructions
   165→│       ├── package.json
   166→│       └── src/app/           # App Router pages and layouts
   167→│
   168→├── packages/
   169→│   ├── shared-types/          # Auto-generated TypeScript types
   170→│   │   └── src/
   171→│   │       ├── schemas.ts     # Zod schemas (manually maintained)
   172→│   │       └── index.ts       # Re-exports everything
   173→│   └── ui/                    # Shared React components
   174→│       └── src/
   175→│           ├── button.tsx
   176→│           └── index.ts
   177→│
   178→└── skills/                    # Executable skill scripts
   179→    ├── SKILLS_REGISTRY.md     # Master index of all skills
   180→    ├── checkpoint/            # Versioned git snapshot
   181→    ├── db-migrate/            # Alembic migration runner
   182→    ├── dependency-add/        # Package installer (enforces pnpm/uv)
   183→    ├── lint-fix/              # Linter + auto-fixer
   184→    ├── memory/                # Agent save/recall memory
   185→    ├── openapi-gen/           # OpenAPI spec exporter
   186→    ├── test-run/              # Test suite runner
   187→    └── type-sync/             # Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript pipeline
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Getting Started
   193→
   194→### Option A — Docker (recommended)
   195→
   196→The fastest way to get a full working stack: FastAPI + PostgreSQL + ChromaDB, all containerised.
   197→
   198→**Prerequisites:** [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/)
   199→
   200→```bash
   201→git clone https://github.com/whyujjwal/AgentOptimisedMonorepo.git
   202→cd AgentOptimisedMonorepo
   203→
   204→# Start everything
   205→bash skills/docker/run.sh up
   206→
   207→# Apply database migrations
   208→bash skills/docker/run.sh migrate
   209→```
   210→
   211→That's it. Services available at:
   212→- **API** → `http://localhost:8000`
   213→- **Swagger UI** → `http://localhost:8000/docs`
   214→- **PostgreSQL** → `localhost:5432` (user: `monorepo`, pass: `monorepo`, db: `monorepo`)
   215→
   216→See the [Docker section](#docker) below for all available commands.
   217→
   218→### Option B — Local dev without Docker
   219→
   220→**Prerequisites:**
   221→
   222→| Tool | Install |
   223→|------|---------|
   224→| Node 20+ | [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org) |
   225→| pnpm 10+ | `npm install -g pnpm` |
   226→| Python 3.11+ | [python.org](https://python.org) |
   227→| uv | `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh \| sh` |
   228→| Git | standard |
   229→
   230→**1. Clone and install:**
   231→
   232→```bash
   233→git clone https://github.com/whyujjwal/AgentOptimisedMonorepo.git
   234→cd AgentOptimisedMonorepo
   235→
   236→# Install all JavaScript/TypeScript packages
   237→pnpm install
   238→
   239→# Install all Python packages
   240→cd apps/api && uv sync && cd ../..
   241→```
   242→
   243→**2. Configure environment:**
   244→
   245→```bash
   246→cp apps/api/.env.example apps/api/.env
   247→# Edit apps/api/.env — SQLite works out of the box, no Postgres config needed
   248→```
   249→
   250→**3. Start all dev servers:**
   251→
   252→```bash
   253→pnpm dev
   254→```
   255→
   256→This starts:
   257→- **Frontend** at `http://localhost:3000`
   258→- **Backend** at `http://localhost:8000`
   259→- **API Docs** at `http://localhost:8000/docs`
   260→
   261→**Or run backend only:**
   262→
   263→```bash
   264→cd apps/api && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload
   265→```
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→## The Stack
   270→
   271→### Backend — FastAPI
   272→
   273→The backend is a **FastAPI** application using Python 3.11+.
   274→
   275→**Key libraries:**
   276→- `fastapi` — HTTP framework with automatic OpenAPI generation
   277→- `pydantic v2` — Data validation and settings management
   278→- `sqlalchemy 2.0` — ORM for relational data
   279→- `alembic` — Database schema migrations
   280→- `chromadb` — Local vector database for agent memory
   281→- `uvicorn` — ASGI server
   282→
   283→**Why FastAPI over Django/Flask?**
   284→FastAPI automatically generates an OpenAPI spec from your Python type hints. This is the engine behind the entire type synchronization pipeline. Change a Pydantic model → the spec updates → TypeScript types update. Zero manual work.
   285→
   286→### Frontend — Next.js
   287→
   288→The frontend uses **Next.js 16** with the **App Router**.
   289→
   290→**Key decisions:**
   291→- **Server components by default** — only add `"use client"` when interactivity requires it. This keeps the bundle small and SSR fast.
   292→- **`@repo/shared-types`** — Never write API response types manually. Import them from the shared package.
   293→- **`@repo/ui`** — Look here before creating a new component.
   294→
   295→### Shared Types Pipeline
   296→
   297→This is the most important architectural feature. Here is how backend and frontend types stay in sync:
   298→
   299→```
   300→FastAPI Pydantic schemas
   301→         │
   302→         ▼
   303→ openapi.json (generated)
   304→         │
   305→         ▼
   306→ packages/shared-types
   307→         │
   308→         ▼
   309→TypeScript interfaces + Zod schemas
   310→         │
   311→         ▼
   312→ apps/web imports @repo/shared-types
   313→```
   314→
   315→**You never need to write TypeScript interfaces for API responses.** They are generated.
   316→
   317→To trigger the full pipeline after a schema change:
   318→
   319→```bash
   320→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   321→```
   322→
   323→### UI Library
   324→
   325→`packages/ui` is a shared React component library. Add components here when they are used by more than one app. Currently exports `<Button />`.
   326→
   327→---
   328→
   329→## The Skills System
   330→
   331→### What Is a Skill?
   332→
   333→A skill is a bash script that encodes a repeatable, multi-step operation into a single command. It is a shared playbook for both humans and AI agents.
   334→
   335→Every skill lives in `skills/<name>/` and contains three files:
   336→
   337→```
   338→skills/my-skill/
   339→├── manifest.json   # Machine-readable: name, trigger, inputs, risk level
   340→├── README.md       # Human/agent-readable: when and why to use it
   341→└── run.sh          # The actual executable, always run from repo root
   342→```
   343→
   344→Agents discover skills by reading `skills/SKILLS_REGISTRY.md` first. Skills replace the need to remember long command sequences.
   345→
   346→### Available Skills
   347→
   348→| Skill | Run Command | When to Use |
   349→|-------|-------------|-------------|
   350→| `type-sync` | `bash skills/type-sync/run.sh` | After changing any Pydantic schema |
   351→| `db-migrate` | `bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "message"` | After changing a SQLAlchemy model |
   352→| `openapi-gen` | `bash skills/openapi-gen/run.sh` | To regenerate `openapi.json` alone |
   353→| `docker` | `bash skills/docker/run.sh up\|down\|migrate` | Start/stop the full stack (API + Postgres) |
   354→| `memory` | `bash skills/memory/run.sh save\|recall\|list` | Save or recall agent context |
   355→| `checkpoint` | `bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "message"` | At logical milestones (commits) |
   356→| `dependency-add` | `bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh js\|py <pkg>` | Adding any new package |
   357→| `lint-fix` | `bash skills/lint-fix/run.sh` | Before committing |
   358→| `test-run` | `bash skills/test-run/run.sh` | After completing work |
   359→
   360→### When Agents Must Use Skills
   361→
   362→These triggers are **mandatory**, not optional:
   363→
   364→```
   365→Changed apps/api/app/schemas/**   →  bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   366→Changed apps/api/app/models/**    →  bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "reason"
   367→Need a new package                →  bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh <lang> <pkg>
   368→Need the local stack running      →  bash skills/docker/run.sh up
   369→Feature complete                  →  bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "description"
   370→Want to remember something        →  bash skills/memory/run.sh save "content"
   371→```
   372→
   373→---
   374→
   375→## Agent Memory — Local Vector Database
   376→
   377→### Why Agents Need Memory
   378→
   379→An AI agent without memory is goldfish-like — every session, it starts from zero. It re-asks questions it has already answered, forgets user preferences it learned last week, re-investigates bugs it already traced.
   380→
   381→Memory changes this. An agent can:
   382→- Record a decision: *"We decided to use UUIDs for all primary keys"*
   383→- Record a user preference: *"User always wants dark mode defaults"*
   384→- Record a debugging discovery: *"The auth bug was caused by a missing await on the token refresh"*
   385→- ...and recall any of this semantically in a future session
   386→
   387→### How It Works Under the Hood
   388→
   389→Memory uses **ChromaDB**, a local vector database that runs entirely in the repo directory. No external API. No account needed. No data leaves your machine.
   390→
   391→When you save a memory:
   392→1. ChromaDB **embeds** your text into a high-dimensional vector using a local embedding model
   393→2. It stores the vector alongside the original text and any metadata in `.data/chromadb/`
   394→3. The data persists on disk across sessions
   395→
   396→When you search:
   397→1. Your query gets embedded into the same vector space
   398→2. ChromaDB finds the **nearest vectors** using cosine similarity
   399→3. Returns results sorted by relevance score (0.0–1.0)
   400→
   401→This is **semantic search** — not keyword matching. You can search for *"user UI preferences"* and find a memory that says *"the product owner wants a minimal, light interface"*, even though none of those words overlap.
   402→
   403→```
   404→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   405→│                    ChromaDB Vector Store                         │
   406→│                                                                  │
   407→│  Memory: "User prefers dark mode"                               │
   408→│  Vector: [0.23, -0.87, 0.41, 0.12, ...]  (768 dimensions)      │
   409→│  Tags:   ["user_42", "preferences"]                             │
   410→│  Time:   2026-02-18T10:30:00                                    │
   411→│                                                                  │
   412→│  Memory: "Auth bug caused by missing await on token refresh"    │
   413→│  Vector: [0.11, 0.34, -0.62, 0.88, ...]                        │
   414→│  Tags:   ["bugs", "auth"]                                       │
   415→│                                                                  │
   416→│  Search: "why did auth break?"                                  │
   417→│  → cosine similarity against all vectors                        │
   418→│  → returns [auth bug memory, score: 0.92]                       │
   419→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   420→```
   421→
   422→### Using Memory As a Developer
   423→
   424→The memory service is importable directly in Python:
   425→
   426→```python
   427→from app.services.memory import MemoryService
   428→
   429→svc = MemoryService()
   430→
   431→# Save something
   432→svc.add(
   433→    "We use UUIDs for all primary keys across the API",
   434→    tags=["architecture", "decisions"],
   435→    metadata={"decided_by": "team", "date": "2026-02-18"},
   436→)
   437→
   438→# Recall semantically
   439→results = svc.search("what type of IDs do we use?")
   440→for r in results:
   441→    print(f"[{r['score']:.0%}] {r['content']}")
   442→
   443→# List by tag
   444→user_prefs = svc.list_memories(tags=["user_42"])
   445→
   446→# Delete
   447→svc.delete(memory_id="abc-123")
   448→```
   449→
   450→### Using Memory As an Agent
   451→
   452→Agents interact via the `memory` skill from anywhere in the repo:
   453→
   454→```bash
   455→# Save a memory (with optional comma-separated tags)
   456→bash skills/memory/run.sh save "The user wants pagination on all list endpoints" "api,ux"
   457→
   458→# Recall memories semantically
   459→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "what did we decide about lists?"
   460→
   461→# List all memories tagged with a specific tag
   462→bash skills/memory/run.sh list "api"
   463→```
   464→
   465→**Example output from `recall`:**
   466→
   467→```
   468→=== Recalling memories ===
   469→Found 2 memories:
   470→
   471→[94% match] The user wants pagination on all list endpoints
   472→  ID: 7f3c1a2b | Tags: api,ux
   473→
   474→[71% match] List endpoints should return total count alongside results
   475→  ID: 9e2b8d4f | Tags: api
   476→```
   477→
   478→### Memory API Endpoints
   479→
   480→The memory system is also exposed over HTTP for any service or tool to use:
   481→
   482→```
   483→POST /memory/add
   484→Body: { "content": "string", "tags": ["string"], "metadata": {} }
   485→
   486→POST /memory/search
   487→Body: { "query": "string", "tags": ["string"], "limit": 10 }
   488→```
   489→
   490→---
   491→
   492→## Multi-Agent Coordination
   493→
   494→Multiple AI agents can work in this repo simultaneously. Without rules, they conflict. Here is the system:
   495→
   496→### Zone Ownership
   497→
   498→Each agent is assigned a zone. Within a zone, an agent operates freely. Crossing zones requires care.
   499→
   500→| Zone | Path | What lives here |
   501→|------|------|-----------------|
   502→| Backend | `apps/api/` | FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, migrations |
   503→| Frontend | `apps/web/` | Next.js pages, components, hooks |
   504→| Shared Types | `packages/shared-types/` | Auto-generated — owned by `type-sync` |
   505→| UI Library | `packages/ui/` | Shared React components |
   506→| Skills | `skills/` | Skill scripts |
   507→
   508→### The Type Contract
   509→
   510→`openapi.json` and `packages/shared-types/src/api-types.ts` are **auto-generated files**. Do not edit them manually. They are owned by the `type-sync` skill.
   511→
   512→The contract is simple:
   513→- Backend agent changes a Pydantic schema → runs `type-sync`
   514→- `type-sync` regenerates `openapi.json` and `api-types.ts`
   515→- Frontend agent imports from `@repo/shared-types` — types are always up to date
   516→
   517→### Commit Conventions
   518→
   519→Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) scoped to zones:
   520→
   521→```
   522→feat(api): add user authentication endpoint
   523→fix(web): correct pagination on search results
   524→chore(deps): bump fastapi to 0.116.0
   525→docs(skills): update memory skill README
   526→refactor(api): extract email validation to service layer
   527→checkpoint: auth feature complete
   528→```
   529→
   530→---
   531→
   532→## Context Versioning with Entire
   533→
   534→[Entire](https://entire.dev) is a CLI that hooks into git and snapshots an AI agent's reasoning alongside every commit. This means you can look back at *why* a change was made, not just *what* changed.
   535→
   536→The `checkpoint` skill wraps this:
   537→
   538→```bash
   539→# Creates a git commit + Entire context snapshot
   540→bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "finished user auth"
   541→```
   542→
   543→**Browse history:**
   544→```bash
   545→entire log              # List all checkpoints
   546→entire diff <id>        # See what changed between checkpoints
   547→```
   548→
   549→---
   550→
   551→## Backend Deep Dive
   552→
   553→### Configuration
   554→
   555→All configuration lives in `app/core/config.py` using **Pydantic Settings**. It reads from environment variables and `.env` files automatically.
   556→
   557→```python
   558→from app.core.config import settings
   559→
   560→print(settings.DATABASE_URL)
   561→print(settings.MEMORY_DB_PATH)
   562→print(settings.DEBUG)
   563→```
   564→
   565→Never hardcode config values. Never use `os.environ.get()` directly. Always go through `settings`.
   566→
   567→### Structured Logging
   568→
   569→The logging system produces human-readable colored output in development and machine-parseable JSON in production.
   570→
   571→```python
   572→from app.core.logging import get_logger
   573→
   574→logger = get_logger(__name__)
   575→
   576→# Basic
   577→logger.info("user signed up")
   578→
   579→# With structured fields (show up as JSON keys in production)
   580→logger.info("payment processed", user_id=42, amount_cents=1999, currency="USD")
   581→
   582→# Warnings and errors
   583→logger.warning("rate limit approaching", requests_remaining=5)
   584→logger.error("payment failed", order_id="abc", exc_info=True)
   585→```
   586→
   587→**Never use `print()`**. It bypasses log levels and structured output.
   588→
   589→Switch to JSON output in production by setting `LOG_JSON=true`.
   590→
   591→### Database Layer
   592→
   593→SQLAlchemy 2.0 with synchronous sessions (async can be added if needed).
   594→
   595→**Defining a model:**
   596→
   597→```python
   598→# apps/api/app/models/user.py
   599→from sqlalchemy import String
   600→from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column
   601→from app.core.database import Base
   602→
   603→class User(Base):
   604→    __tablename__ = "users"
   605→
   606→    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
   607→    email: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), unique=True)
   608→    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100))
   609→```
   610→
   611→**Import it** in `app/models/__init__.py` so Alembic can discover it:
   612→
   613→```python
   614→from app.models.user import User  # noqa: F401
   615→```
   616→
   617→**Create the migration:**
   618→
   619→```bash
   620→bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "add users table"
   621→```
   622→
   623→**Using the DB in routes:**
   624→
   625→```python
   626→from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
   627→from fastapi import Depends
   628→from app.core.database import get_db
   629→
   630→@router.get("/users")
   631→def list_users(db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
   632→    return db.query(User).all()
   633→```
   634→
   635→### Adding an Endpoint
   636→
   637→Here is the full workflow for a new endpoint:
   638→
   639→**1. Define the Pydantic schemas** in `app/schemas/`:
   640→
   641→```python
   642→# app/schemas/user.py
   643→from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr
   644→
   645→class UserCreateRequest(BaseModel):
   646→    email: str
   647→    name: str
   648→
   649→class UserResponse(BaseModel):
   650→    id: int
   651→    email: str
   652→    name: str
   653→```
   654→
   655→**2. Add them to** `app/schemas/__init__.py`:
   656→
   657→```python
   658→from app.schemas.user import UserCreateRequest, UserResponse
   659→__all__ = [..., "UserCreateRequest", "UserResponse"]
   660→```
   661→
   662→**3. Write the route** in `app/api/routes/users.py`:
   663→
   664→```python
   665→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
   666→from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
   667→from app.core.database import get_db
   668→from app.core.logging import get_logger
   669→from app.schemas.user import UserCreateRequest, UserResponse
   670→from app.models.user import User
   671→
   672→logger = get_logger(__name__)
   673→router = APIRouter(prefix="/users")
   674→
   675→@router.post("/", response_model=UserResponse)
   676→def create_user(req: UserCreateRequest, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
   677→    user = User(email=req.email, name=req.name)
   678→    db.add(user)
   679→    db.commit()
   680→    logger.info("user created", email=req.email)
   681→    return user
   682→```
   683→
   684→**4. Register the router** in `app/api/routes/__init__.py`:
   685→
   686→```python
   687→from app.api.routes import health, memory, users
   688→
   689→api_router.include_router(users.router, tags=["users"])
   690→```
   691→
   692→**5. Sync the types:**
   693→
   694→```bash
   695→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   696→```
   697→
   698→Done. The frontend now has TypeScript types for `UserCreateRequest` and `UserResponse`.
   699→
   700→---
   701→
   702→## Frontend Deep Dive
   703→
   704→The frontend follows Next.js 15+ App Router conventions.
   705→
   706→**Use types from shared package:**
   707→
   708→```typescript
   709→import type { HealthResponse } from "@repo/shared-types";
   710→
   711→async function fetchHealth(): Promise<HealthResponse> {
   712→  const res = await fetch("http://localhost:8000/health");
   713→  return res.json();
   714→}
   715→```
   716→
   717→**Use components from UI library:**
   718→
   719→```typescript
   720→import { Button } from "@repo/ui";
   721→
   722→export default function Page() {
   723→  return <Button variant="primary">Click me</Button>;
   724→}
   725→```
   726→
   727→**Pages go in** `src/app/` following App Router conventions:
   728→
   729→```
   730→src/app/
   731→├── layout.tsx          # Root layout (html, body, providers)
   732→├── page.tsx            # Home page (/)
   733→├── users/
   734→│   ├── page.tsx        # /users
   735→│   └── [id]/page.tsx   # /users/:id
   736→```
   737→

   739→
   740→## Development Workflow
   741→
   742→### Daily workflow for a feature
   743→
   744→```bash
   745→# 1. Start dev servers
   746→pnpm dev
   747→
   748→# 2. Make changes to backend schema
   749→# edit apps/api/app/schemas/user.py
   750→
   751→# 3. Sync types to frontend automatically
   752→bash skills/type-sync/run.sh
   753→
   754→# 4. Add a new package if needed
   755→bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh py httpx
   756→bash skills/dependency-add/run.sh js zod web
   757→
   758→# 5. Save important decisions to agent memory
   759→bash skills/memory/run.sh save "User endpoints use cursor-based pagination" "api,decisions"
   760→
   761→# 6. Fix linting
   762→bash skills/lint-fix/run.sh
   763→
   764→# 7. Run tests
   765→bash skills/test-run/run.sh
   766→
   767→# 8. Commit and checkpoint
   768→bash skills/checkpoint/run.sh "add user endpoints with pagination"
   769→```
   770→
   771→### Adding a migration
   772→
   773→```bash
   774→# After modifying any file in apps/api/app/models/
   775→bash skills/db-migrate/run.sh "add email_verified column to users"
   776→```
   777→
   778→### Recalling past decisions
   779→
   780→```bash
   781→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "pagination approach"
   782→bash skills/memory/run.sh recall "why we use UUIDs"
   783→bash skills/memory/run.sh list "decisions"
   784→```

   787→
   788→## Docker
   789→
   790→The monorepo ships with a production-ready Docker setup. One command starts the entire stack.
   791→
   792→### Services
   793→
   794→| Service | Image | Port | Volume |
   795→|---------|-------|------|--------|
   796→| `api` | Built from `apps/api/Dockerfile` | 8000 | `chromadb_data:/app/.data/chromadb` |
   797→| `postgres` | `postgres:16-alpine` | 5432 | `postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data` |
   798→
   799→Both services have health checks. The API waits for Postgres to be healthy before starting.
   800→
   801→### Docker Commands
   802→
   803→```bash
   804→# Start everything (builds images if needed)
   805→bash skills/docker/run.sh up
   806→
   807→# Run Alembic migrations inside the API container
   808→bash skills/docker/run.sh migrate
   809→
   810→# Tail all logs
   811→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs
   812→
   813→# Tail logs for one service
   814→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs api
   815→bash skills/docker/run.sh logs postgres
   816→
   817→# Stop containers (data volumes preserved)
   818→bash skills/docker/run.sh down
   819→
   820→# Rebuild images without starting
   821→bash skills/docker/run.sh build
   822→
   823→# Open an interactive shell inside the API container
   824→bash skills/docker/run.sh shell
   825→
   826→# ⚠ Destroy everything including volumes (all data gone)
   827→bash skills/docker/run.sh reset
   828→```
   829→
   830→### Connecting to Postgres
   831→
   832→**Via Docker:**
   833→```bash
   834→docker compose exec postgres psql -U monorepo -d monorepo
   835→```
   836→
   837→**From a local client (port 5432 is exposed):**
   838→```
   839→postgresql://monorepo:monorepo@localhost:5432/monorepo
   840→```
   841→
   842→To use a different database name or credentials, copy `.env.example` → `.env` and edit:
   843→```bash
   844→cp .env.example .env
   845→# Then edit POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DB
   846→```
   847→
   848→---
   849→
   850→## Environment Variables Reference
   851→
   852→**`apps/api/.env`** — for running the backend locally (copy from `apps/api/.env.example`):
   853→
   854→| Variable | Default | Description |
   855→|----------|---------|-------------|
   856→| `APP_NAME` | `AI-Native Monorepo API` | Displayed in API docs |
   857→| `APP_VERSION` | `0.1.0` | Displayed in API docs |
   858→| `DEBUG` | `false` | Enables SQLAlchemy query logging |
   859→| `DATABASE_URL` | `sqlite:///./dev.db` | SQLAlchemy connection string |
   860→| `MEMORY_DB_PATH` | `.data/chromadb` | Path for ChromaDB vector store |
   861→| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | Logging threshold (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR) |
   862→| `LOG_JSON` | `false` | Set `true` in production for JSON log output |
   863→
   864→**`.env`** — for Docker Compose (copy from `.env.example` at the repo root):
   865→
   866→| Variable | Default | Description |
   867→|----------|---------|-------------|
   868→| `POSTGRES_USER` | `monorepo` | Postgres username |
   869→| `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` | `monorepo` | Postgres password |
   870→| `POSTGRES_DB` | `monorepo` | Postgres database name |
   871→| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | API log level inside Docker |
   872→| `DEBUG` | `false` | API debug mode inside Docker |
   873→
   874→For production `DATABASE_URL`:
   875→```
   876→DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg2://user:pass@host:5432/dbname
   877→```
